candidates.name: Abigail Spanberger
candidates.notes: Governor of Virginia (D), sworn Jan 17, 2026 (75th Gov, first woman); won 2025 race ~57% over Lt. Gov. Earle-Sears. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Progressive on social/cultural issues with centrist-Dem posture on policing and a Blue Dog fiscal record. Heritage Action 5%. Pro-abortion: cosponsored + voted YES (twice) on the federal Women's Health Protection Act; signed SB449 Feb 6 2026 sending VA reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment to Nov 2026 ballot; signed contraceptive-access bills April 2026; endorsed by Planned Parenthood, EMILYs List, Reproductive Freedom for All. Pro-same-sex-marriage: voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act 2022 (twice); signed HB612 marriage-amendment ballot referral; original cosponsor Equality Act; HRC endorsement. Anti-school-choice: campaign platform 'oppose efforts that take public dollars out of public schools'; 'get culture wars out of our schools.' Pro-gun-control: cosponsored + voted YES on Assault Weapons Ban 2022 (HR1808); signed VA SB749/HB217 assault-weapons ban May 14–15 2026. Pro-mass-mail-in voting / opposes SAVE Act citizenship verification. Anti-sanctuary-cooperation: Executive Directive 1 (Feb 4 2026) terminated all VA 287(g) agreements (State Police, DOC, Wildlife Resources, Marine Resources); voted NO on HR2 Secure the Border Act 2023. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: famous Nov 2020 caucus-call quote 'No one should say defund the police ever again'; co-led bipartisan Invest to Protect Act for police funding (PolitiFact rated false the GOP claim she backs defunding); Iron Dome funding YES; bipartisan Social Security Fairness Act signed into law.

profile.religion: Protestant (unspecified) — no specific congregation publicly stated
profile.education: University of Virginia (BA French Literature), Purdue/GISMA Business School (MBA)
profile.background: 75th Governor of Virginia (first woman); sworn in Jan 17, 2026. Former US Rep VA-07 (2019–2025, 3 terms; did not seek reelection 2024). Prior career: CIA case officer (counterterrorism); US Postal Inspector. Blue Dog Coalition + New Democrat + Problem Solvers Caucus member in the House.

china.note: $50,000 from Pin Ni (CCP member, Wanxiang) in two $25,000 installments April + May 2025, supporting Virginia gubernatorial bid. Pin is named in Chinese state media 15 times as 'outstanding Communist Party member.'
china.descriptor: China-linked donor tracking. The CCP operates an explicit United Front Work Department to direct overseas members and proxies — including business executives such as Wanxiang Group's Pin Ni (named CCP member 15 times in Chinese state media) — to cultivate political relationships in the United States. Federal law prohibits CCP members from donating to U.S. campaigns, and most CCP-member donors hold green cards rather than citizenship, raising significant legal questions. RESOLUTE Citizen treats any documented contribution from a CCP-member or United Front-affiliated donor as a failure on america_first/q3.

candidates.name: Ghazala Hashmi
candidates.notes: Lt. Governor of Virginia (D), sworn Jan 17, 2026; first Muslim woman elected statewide in US. Prior: VA Senate SD-15 (Richmond/Chesterfield) 2020-2026, Education & Health Chair. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Solidly progressive across the rubric. Patron of SJ247 (2025 reproductive-freedom amendment) AND SJ249 (2025 same-sex-marriage amendment). Sponsored SB280 (2024 'Death with Dignity' physician-assisted suicide; passed Senate 21-19) and SB1098 (2025 shield law for out-of-state abortion + gender-medicine providers). As Ed/Health Chair, panel killed 2023 parental-rights bills on school books and student gender-identity notification, and rejected charter-school expansion. Voted for Virginia Values Act 2020 (state SOGI nondiscrimination, no conscience carve-out). Patron of SB5035 (2020 spec session) civilian police-oversight boards with binding disciplinary authority including termination. Sponsored 'Cover All Kids' Medicaid-like coverage for undocumented children. Voted YES on SB1 (2025) semi-auto firearms + high-capacity-mag ban (Youngkin-vetoed). Endorsed by Planned Parenthood, Reproductive Freedom for All, ReproRising VA PAC, Giffords, Everytown ('gun sense champion'), Brady, VA LGBT+ Democrats. June 2026 (as LG) publicly attacked Commonwealth's Attorneys who declined to enforce the new assault-weapons ban. No cross-party deviations found.

Sargeant Reynolds Community College, founding director of Center for Excellence in Teaching & Learning. Prior office: VA State Senate SD-15 (2020–Jan 2026); chaired Senate Education & Health Committee 2024–2026.

candidates.name: Jay Jones
candidates.notes: Attorney General of Virginia (D), sworn Jan 17, 2026 (49th AG, first Black AG); won 2025 race 53-46 vs R incumbent Miyares. Prior: VA House Delegate HD-89 (Norfolk) 2018-2022. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Reliably progressive record on the marquee cells: self-confirmed past board member of 'Virginia Planned Parenthood'; co-patron 2019 'Repeal Act' (HB2491) removing ultrasound + 24-hour-wait abortion restrictions; PP Advocates VA + Reproductive Freedom For All + ReproRising VA PAC + Roe Your Vote endorsements; Aug 2025 hosted 'Reproductive Freedom Roundtable' with PP. Voted YES on Virginia Values Act 2020 (LGBTQ nondiscrimination, no conscience carve-out); VA LGBT+ Democrats endorsement. Co-sponsored/voted YES on 2020 Dem omnibus gun package; Everytown $200K + endorsement (later scrubbed after texts surfaced); Giffords + Brady + Prevent Gun Tragedies PAC endorsements; as AG-elect Dec 2025 intervened to salvage VA's universal background-check law on appeal. Anti-sanctuary-ban: 2018 voted NO on HB1257 (would have banned sanctuary jurisdictions); Day-1 as AG announced review of Miyares opinion authorizing ICE detainers. CRITICAL EVIDENCE — 2022 text scandal: Aug 2022 text exchange with R Del. Carrie Coyner surfaced October 2025 (National Review): Jones wrote of then-Speaker Todd Gilbert 'Three people, two bullets / Gilbert, hitler, and pol pot / Gilbert gets two bullets to the head' and that Gilbert's children were 'breeding little fascists' he hoped would die because 'Only when people feel pain personally do they move on policy,' and that he'd 'go to their funerals to piss on their graves.' Coyner replied: 'Please stop … It really bothers me when you talk about hurting people or wishing death on them.' Jones publicly apologized ('reading back those words made me sick to my stomach'). Coyner separately alleges a 2020 phone call where Jones said about police 'Well, maybe if a few of them died, that they would move on, not shooting people, not killing people' — contested by Jones. Spanberger called the comments 'disgusting.' Jones was co-patron of 2020 qualified-immunity ban with floor quote 'take a whole bunch of frivolous lawsuits if it means that someone who is shot 7 times in the back gets a day in court.'

profile.religion: Roman Catholic — Basilica of St. Mary of the Immaculate Conception, Norfolk
profile.education: College of William & Mary (BA Government & History), University of Virginia School of Law (JD)
profile.background: 49th Attorney General of Virginia, sworn Jan 17, 2026 by his mother (Norfolk Juvenile & Domestic Relations Chief Judge Lyn M. Simmons); first Black AG of Virginia. Prior office: VA House of Delegates HD-89 (Norfolk), Jan 10, 2018 – Jan 2, 2022. Self-confirmed past member of 'Virginia Planned Parenthood board.' Beat incumbent R Jason Miyares 53.14%-46.45% Nov 4, 2025.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Strong conservative HARD record, no deviations: pro-life (voted NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment SJ247/HJR1, on-record parental-consent objection; ReproRising 2025 grade F; sponsored SB163 barring abortion-requiring surrogacy clauses); held the marriage line (voted NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment with explicit faith rationale — 'my faith teaches me marriage is between one man and one woman' — NOT a crossover, unlike Durant); strong parental-rights advocacy; CHIEF PATRON of voter-photo-ID and registration-verification bills (SB168, SB1070); pro-2A (on record: the assault-weapons ban is 'a direct infringement on the Second Amendment'). NOTE: he resigned the RPV CHAIRMANSHIP 12/31/2025 but remains the sitting SD-8 senator. Economic-stewardship, public-justice, federal-overreach, border, and christian-liberty cells left NULL — no itemized Peake-specific record found (VPAP donor page 403). Sourcing note: lis.virginia.gov JS-rendered; named votes corroborated via VPM, Virginia Mercury, Ballotpedia News, ReproRising PDF, Wikipedia, official Senate member page.

candidates.name: Tammy Brankley Mulchi
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-9 (sitting since Jan 2024 special election). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent conservative HARD record (richest roll call of this batch, via Richmond Sunlight mirror of the official feed): pro-life (voted NO on SJ247/HJ1 2025 and HJ1/SJ1 2026 reproductive-freedom amendments, plus SB754, SB743; ReproRising 2025 grade F); held the marriage line (voted NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment in BOTH 2025 and 2026 — not among the GOP crossovers); affirms biological sex (chief patron of SB723/SB749 women's-sports bills and SJ49 'affirming biological differences between the sexes'); tough-on-crime victim/penalty sponsorships (SB673 stalking, SB700 repeat prostitution-solicitation penalties, SB616/SB653 protective orders, SB844 sex-offender registry) plus SB1420 (forfeited assets for law-enforcement use); strongly pro-2A (voted NO across the entire 2025-26 gun-control slate — assault-firearms ban SB1181, magazine limits, waiting period, mandatory storage — and YES on the SB1389 safety-device tax credit). Election-integrity left NULL: she cast many votes on Democratic-majority election bills but the per-bill direction could not be verified, so no integrity signal is scored (honest null). Christian-liberty, federal-overreach, border, family-choice, and economic CBDC/ESG cells left NULL. Sourcing note: VPAP/new LIS API/legiscan unreachable; roll calls from Richmond Sunlight (Open-Virginia mirror of the official Legislature feed), crossover list from Ballotpedia News.

candidates.name: Creigh Deeds
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-11 (sitting since 2001). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Long-tenured mainstream Senate Democrat. Pro-abortion-rights HARD record: named 2023 'Reproductive Freedom Champion' by ReproRising (formerly NARAL), endorsed by the group, in the Democratic majority advancing the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247/HJ1). Marriage: in the majority advancing the same-sex-marriage amendment (2025 & 2026 references) and publicly opposed Youngkin's transgender-student model policy. Opposed school choice/ESAs ('nonstarter… defund public schools') and voter ID (urged a 2012 veto). Economic: backs the $15 minimum wage. Public justice: sponsored bail reform (SB1266) and voted to abolish the death penalty (2021). Guns: sponsored an assault-weapons ban (SB1382), backed universal background checks/safe-storage; GIFFORDS-endorsed. CROSS-CURRENT (historical, noted not scored): in 2009 voted against in-state tuition/benefits for undocumented immigrants and for English-as-official-language — restrictionist votes atypical of his caucus, but 2009-era and not mapping to the current border cells. Several amendment roll-call lines are party-line-majority inferences (VPAP blocked automated read) but his alignment is independently corroborated by HARD endorsements/statements. No conservative deviations on the scored cells.

profile.religion: Presbyterian (member, Presbyterian Church)
profile.background: VA State Senator since 2001 (House of Delegates 1992-2001); attorney, former Bath County Commonwealth's Attorney; 2005 AG nominee and 2009 Democratic gubernatorial nominee. SD-11 is a sprawling central/western VA district (Bath, Alleghany, Charlottesville/Albemarle area) after redistricting from the old SD-25. Virginia's leading mental-health-reform legislator following his son's 2013 death.
profile.education: Concord College (BA, 1980), Wake Forest University School of Law (JD, 1984)

candidates.name: Glen Sturtevant
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-12 (returned to the Senate 2024; prior service 2016-2020). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent Christian-conservative record. Life: self-described '100% pro-life,' graded F by ReproRising 2025, opposed the reproductive-freedom amendment with the GOP bloc. Marriage (VERIFIED, the key cell): voted NO on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026) — the ONLY 5 Republican crossovers were DeSteph, Diggs, Durant, Jordan, and Suetterlein; Sturtevant was in the 13-member NO bloc, did NOT cross. Family: backs school choice ('money follows the child') and sponsored parental content-opt-out legislation. Elections: supports restoring photo voter ID. Immigration: restrictionist; sponsored an ICE-transfer bill for incarcerated persons. Guns: opposed the 2026 mandatory safe-storage bill and argued to repeal the red-flag law. ONE DOCUMENTED RECORD DEVIATION (noted, not scored): voted FOR red-flag legislation in 2019 but now favors repeal — his current position is opposition, which is what's scored. Cells with no public evidence (christian liberty, federal overreach, CBDC/ESG, death-penalty, a numeric Giffords grade) are left null, NOT scored from party.

Served the old SD-10 2016-2020, lost in 2019, returned to the Senate in 2024 after winning the 2023 primary and general. He and his wife are raising four adopted children.

candidates.name: Michael Jones
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-15 (sitting since Jan 2026; former Delegate HD-77; former Richmond City Council President). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] HONEST KEY FINDING: Jones is a Christian pastor (Village of Faith Ministries), but his LIS-verified recorded votes are uniformly progressive — pastoral identity did NOT produce a socially conservative voting record, and he is scored on his votes, not his vocation. Life: co-patron + YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1/SJ247 2025; HB781/SB449 2026); ReproRising A+. Marriage: YES on HJ9 (2025) repealing the one-man/one-woman definition and YES on HB612/SB311 (2026). Economic: YES on multiple minimum-wage bills and paid family leave. Elections: YES on the felon-voting-rights amendment (HJ2) and on expanding in-person absentee voting/registration. Public justice: introduced bills banning tear gas/rubber bullets/chokeholds and voted for marijuana-offense resentencing. Guns: chief patron of an auto-sears ban (HB22) and YES on the assault-firearms/high-capacity-magazine ban (HB1607); GIFFORDS-endorsed. Across ~2,094 recorded 2025 House votes he cast only 3 idiosyncratic NO votes, none a conservative break. christian_liberty (a church-property tax-exemption YES does not map to the symbol/prayer/conscience cells), refuse_federal_overreach, school choice, and sanctuary cells left null.

profile.religion: Christian; founding Senior Pastor of Village of Faith Ministries (non-denominational, Richmond area). M.Div., Samuel DeWitt Proctor School of Theology (Virginia Union); D.Min., McCormick Theological Seminary
profile.background: VA State Senator for SD-15 (parts of Richmond City and Chesterfield County) since Jan 2026; former Delegate HD-77 (2024-2026) and Richmond City Council member/President (2016-2023). Full-time pastor and Democrat; won the Jan 2026 special election for the seat vacated when Ghazala Hashmi became Lt. Governor.

candidates.name: Schuyler VanValkenburg
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-16 (Henrico); sitting since Jan 2024; term runs through 2028. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record. Co-patron + YEA on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247 2025 / HJ1 2026); ReproRising 2025 scorecard A+ ('Champion'); ChoiceTracker 'Pro-Choice' (Planned Parenthood Action endorsed). YEA on the same-sex-marriage amendment HJ3 (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026). Vocal opponent of Education Savings Accounts / school choice. Lead sponsor of legislation returning VA to the ERIC multistate voter-data partnership; in the Democratic majority that advanced felon-voting restoration (HJ2). In the Senate Democratic bloc that passed the 2026 ICE-cooperation restrictions (21-19 party line) and the $15 minimum-wage law; championed the 2026 gun-reform package (assault-weapons ban, magazine limit, red-flag expansion). No conservative deviations found. RECORD CORRECTION: SB1031 (homeschool religious-exemption repeal) belongs to Sen. Pekarsky, NOT VanValkenburg — do not attribute it to him.

profile.background: VA State Senator, SD-16 (Henrico County), sitting since Jan 2024; defeated incumbent Siobhan Dunnavant Nov 2023; former Delegate HD-72 (2018-2024). History/U.S. Government teacher at Glen Allen HS, National Board Certified; the only public-school teacher in the Senate. B.A. History (Univ. of Richmond), M.A. History (VCU); father of three.

candidates.name: Emily Jordan
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-17, sitting since Jan 2024 (former Delegate HD-64). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Genuinely conservative HARD record (Family Foundation report card 95% pro-family, 18 of 19 votes) with ONE notable deviation. KEY DEVIATION (verified, LIS roll call): voted YES on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026), one of ~5 Republicans crossing over — biblical_marriage q0/q1 FALSE. On the same day she held the conservative line on the other two amendments: NO on HJ1 (abortion) and NO on HJ2 (felon voting), so the marriage vote is a deliberate, isolated break, not a moderate drift. Pro-life voting (NO HJ1); pro-parental-notification (NO on SB19/HB1499 narrowing the explicit-material notice law; YES SB817; YES SB778 obscene-material-to-minor penalty); YES HB131 (religious accommodations in higher ed); fiscal-conservative (NO on the $15 minimum wage SB1); election-integrity (NO on expanded Sunday absentee SB438, NO on felon restoration); pro-police (NO on SB352 restricting LEO, YES SB55/SB111 penalties, NO on SB60 parole expansion); anti-sanctuary (NO on SB783/HB1441 ICE-restriction bills); strongly pro-2A (NO on assault-weapons ban SB749, red-flag SB495, storage mandate SB348).

profile.background: VA State Senator, SD-17, sitting since Jan 2024; former Delegate HD-64 (2018-2024). Small-business owner (wine shop/restaurant), first-responder trainer; Windsor Ruritan. Undergrad Regent University. Married (née Brewer).

candidates.name: L. Louise Lucas
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-18; Senate President pro tempore; Chair, Finance & Appropriations; in the GA since 1992. candidates.name: Danica Roem
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-30; first openly transgender U.S. state senator. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record and an active leader of the measures most opposed by this rubric. Life: sponsor of the reproductive-freedom amendment SJR247; pledges to protect abortion/IVF access. Marriage: on-record 'long overdue that we repealed Marshall Newman… affirmative right to marry'; sponsor/patron of the SSM-repeal amendment (HJ3, Senate 26-13, Jan 16 2026); championed pro-transgender measures; opposes school parental-notification-on-gender bills; ended anti-trans insurance discrimination (2020). 2A: Giffords-endorsed; on-record 'because of my support, we have a red flag law'; votes for UBC + ERPO. Election/justice/border/federalism/economic cells left null (no public record located on those specific items). VPAP donor itemization (abortion/gun PACs) blocked to automated fetch — endorsements documented, dollar figures not quoted.

profile.religion: Raised Catholic; self-describes as 'non-practicing'/'fallen away' but still identifies Catholic (secondary report)
profile.background: Virginia State Senator, District 30 (Prince William/Manassas/Manassas Park); in the Senate since 2024, previously House of Delegates 2018-2024. Former local journalist. Nationally notable as the first openly transgender person elected and seated in a U.S. state legislature, and the first openly transgender state senator in the U.S. South.
profile.education: Paul VI Catholic High School, St. Bonaventure University (BA Journalism, 2006)

candidates.name: Russet Perry
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-31; former CIA officer and Loudoun County prosecutor. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Progressive on the social dimensions with one genuine, documented conservative exception on public justice. Life: co-patron of SJR247/HJ1 (reproductive-freedom amendment); ReproRising A+ 'Champion'; opposes abortion bans even with rape/incest exceptions. Marriage: co-patron of SJR249 (same-sex-marriage repeal amendment, Senate 24-15). 2A: Giffords + Moms Demand endorsed; campaigned on magazine limits and an assault-weapons ban ('weapons of war… don't belong in the hands of a civilian'). Immigration: opposed SB1141 (mandatory transfer of incarcerated persons to ICE) — protected local discretion. PUBLIC-JUSTICE CREDIT (earned, cited): as a former prosecutor she was the SOLE Senate Democrat to vote for the fentanyl felony-homicide bill (Courts of Justice, Jan 2024), siding with Republicans and rejecting the public-health-only framing ('those trafficking in fentanyl should be held accountable'). Net documented score reflects real cross-pressure, not a party default. Christian-liberty/economic/federalism/family cells null (no cited record).

profile.religion: Christian (per official Senate member page; denomination not specified)
profile.background: Virginia State Senator, District 31 (Loudoun/Fauquier); in the Senate since 2024. Former CIA officer and former assistant Commonwealth's Attorney (prosecutor) in Loudoun County. George Washington University + Georgetown Law. Committees: Courts of Justice; Education & Health; Agriculture; Privileges & Elections.

candidates.name: Jennifer Carroll Foy
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-33; sitting since Jan 2024; former Delegate; public-defender background. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive record. SANCTITY OF LIFE: voted YEA on the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJR247, 21-19, Jan 21 2025); patron of SB15 (abortion-shield) and contraceptive-coverage bills; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA and EMILY's List (2019 'Rising Star'). MARRIAGE: in the Democratic YEA bloc on the same-sex-marriage amendment (SJR249/HJR3); publicly championed it. ECONOMICS: chief co-patron of minimum-wage increases, filed a right-to-work-repeal bill (Dec 2025). PUBLIC JUSTICE: documented cash-bail-reform advocate ('justice-for-profit'). IMMIGRATION: opposed GOP enforcement bills (warned against deputizing local police as ICE agents); Dream Act / driver-privilege-card support. SELF-DEFENSE: Giffords-endorsed; patron of SB27 (gun-industry liability, signed 2026), SB364 (gun-violence-prevention center), SB797 (purchase-permit). Family/child-sovereignty, Christian-liberty, federal-overreach dimensions = NO public record, left NULL. One within-party nuance: initially abstained then supported an earned-sentence-credit bill (not a cross-party deviation).

candidates.name: Scott Surovell
candidates.notes: VA Senate Majority Leader, SD-34; in the Senate since 2010. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Strongly progressive, well-documented leadership record. SANCTITY OF LIFE: ReproRising 2025 'A+ Champion'; voted YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJ247). MARRIAGE: voted YES on the same-sex-marriage amendment (SJ249); chief sponsor of Virginia's 2020 conversion-therapy ban for minors. ELECTIONS: vocal floor proponent of repealing Virginia's voter-photo-ID requirement (2020). PUBLIC JUSTICE: chief patron of the 2021 death-penalty abolition (SB1165, Senate 21-17); led the 2020 policing-reform effort and ended jury-sentencing. IMMIGRATION: sponsored ICE-restriction bills and 2020 driver-privilege cards for undocumented residents. SELF-DEFENSE: Giffords champion; chief patron of SB436 (2020 voluntary do-not-sell-firearms list) and co-patron of the 2020 red-flag law. ONE GENUINE CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (noted, NOT scored as alignment): in 2020 he voted in committee AGAINST advancing an assault-weapons ban — but he stated this was strategic/timing and that he supports banning assault weapons, so it does not represent a pro-2A position. Family/child-sovereignty, Christian-liberty, federal-overreach = NO public record, NULL.

profile.background: VA State Senator, SD-34 (City of Fairfax + Fairfax County); Senate Majority Leader (since Jan 2024) and Judiciary chair; in the Senate since a Jan 2010 special election. 12th-generation Virginian; JD, UVA Law; trial attorney.

candidates.name: Dave Marsden
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-35 (Fairfax); in the Senate since 2010; corrections/juvenile-justice career. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Best-documented record in this batch — individual roll-calls pulled from the LIS vote API. candidates.name: Adele McClure
candidates.notes: VA Delegate HD-2 (Arlington/NoVA), sitting since Jan 2024. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Documented progressive record: sponsored/voted YES on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJR1/SJR247) and contraception-access bills; voted NO on restricting public abortion funding (HB404); EMILY's List 2025 award + REPRO Rising endorsement. Co-patron + YES on HJ9 (repeal same-sex-marriage ban); campaign pledges to expand statewide LGBTQ+ protections. Public-education-funding advocate (opposes diverting funds to school choice). Reform-oriented on justice ('supportive, not punitive' responses). Strongly pro-gun-control: sponsored mandatory firearm-locking-device bill (HB2064/HB158) and the dating-partner-loophole bill (HB1869); Giffords/Brady/Moms Demand endorsed. No conservative deviations found. Score 0/100 — all scored cells documented contrary to the rubric; thinner dimensions (Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration, much of elections) left null for lack of a named vote.

profile.religion: Listed as Baptist on the Wikipedia infobox; no first-person faith statement located — treat as unconfirmed.

Director of the VA Legislative Black Caucus; prior eviction-prevention and election-administration roles. EMILY's List 2025 Gabrielle Giffords Rising Star Award recipient.

candidates.name: Alfonso Lopez
candidates.notes: VA Delegate HD-3 (Arlington/Alexandria), sitting since 2012; founder of the VA Latino Caucus; chairs Agriculture/Natural Resources. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Deep, uniformly progressive record across nearly every dimension. Sanctity: NARAL 100% lifetime, co-patron 2020 Reproductive Health Protection Act. Marriage: recorded YEA on HJ9 (2025) and HJ3 (2026) same-sex-marriage amendment; chief patron of VA Human Rights Act expansion + an LGBTQ advisory board. Schools: chief patron HB913 reversing the transgender-student model policies; opposes charter schools. Justice: bans on no-knock warrants/chokeholds, limited mandatory minimums, helped abolish the death penalty. Immigration (his signature): DREAM Act, driver-privilege cards (HB911), anti-ICE bills HB1440/HB1441; clearly pro-immigrant/sanctuary-aligned. Guns: chief patron of concealed-permit-competence (HB916) + firearm tax bills; backs universal background checks. NO conservative deviation. Score 0/100. His 'resist federal authority' work is ANTI-ICE (opposite valence) so federal-overreach cells are NOT credited — left null. Christian-liberty/CBDC/ESG/E-Verify/birthright/IVF have no public record — null.

profile.religion: Not publicly stated (received the Virginia Interfaith Center's Legislator of the Year in 2014, but his own faith/denomination is not disclosed in available sources).
profile.background: VA House of Delegates, District 3 (S. Arlington / N. Alexandria); in office continuously since 2012 (formerly HD-49). First Latino Democrat elected to the General Assembly; founder of the Virginia Latino Caucus; former House Democratic Whip. A.B. Vassar; J.D. Tulane. Father emigrated from Venezuela undocumented — drives his signature immigration focus.
profile.office: Room 1014, General Assembly Building 201 N. 9th St., Richmond, VA 23219

candidates.name: Delores Riley Oates
candidates.notes: VA Delegate HD-31 (Shenandoah Valley), sitting since Jan 2024. [2026-06-02 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly conservative HARD record verified from individual LIS roll-call vote records (her personal Yea/Nay codes, 2025 SessionID 57 + 2026 SessionID 59) plus chief-patron bills and direct statements. CROSS-PARTY CHECK: she did NOT cross over (unlike Tara Durant) — voted NAY on the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ9 2025, HJ3 2026) and NAY on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1 2025+2026). Pro-life ('human life begins at conception'); chief patron of women's-sports/biological-sex bills (HB1809/HB1242); supports school choice + homeschool tax credits; YEA on National Day of Prayer (SJ59) and religious accommodations (HB131); tax-relief and anti-minimum-wage votes; chief patron of voter-ID tightening (HB2402/HB1213); pro-police and anti-cashless-bail votes; opposed anti-ICE-cooperation bills (HB1441); consistent pro-2A NAY votes on the entire gun-control package. Thin cells (federal overreach, several sub-topics) left null — credit held to documented votes, not inferred from party.

candidates.name: Terry Kilgore
candidates.notes: VA Delegate, HD-45 (far SW Virginia), sitting since 1994; House Republican Leader. candidates.name: Stacey Carroll
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Freshman D; individual 2026 floor votes confirmed via the LIS member-vote API (member H0388) -> HARD roll-call evidence. Uniformly progressive scorecard record with no conservative deviation. Voted YES on HJ1 (repro amendment) and the companion repro bills; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA, EMILY's List, and REPRO Rising. Voted YES on HJ3 (marriage amendment) and companion marriage bills. Voted YES on the full 2026 gun-control package (HB217/SB749 assault-firearm & magazine ban, HB1525 firearm restrictions, red-flag expansion). Voted YES on HB1441 (sanctuary-leaning bill restricting local-federal 287(g) immigration cooperation). On elections, voted to kill a photo-ID bill (HB1131) and for expanded absentee/early/Sunday voting. Voted YES on HB1499 (narrowing parental notification on sexually explicit instructional material / barring its use to remove library books). Her bipartisan votes were 90+ consensus items (city charters, behavioral-crisis LE agreements), not scorecard-direction breaks. Public-justice cells left null: her LE record is consensus bills + Democratic police-oversight bills (HB1476, HB1482), no affirmative pro-police advocacy meeting the high bar and no defund vote either.

candidates.name: Keith Hodges
candidates.notes: VA Delegate, HD-68 (sitting since 2011/2012; longtime member). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent conservative HARD record from official VA LIS member-vote API (MemberID 180), 2025 & 2026 Regular Sessions. SANCTITY: voted NO on both references of the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1 2025 first ref 51-48; HJ1/SJ1 2026 second ref) and NO on contraception-right bills (HB6, SB596) -> consistent pro-life voting. MARRIAGE: voted NO on the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment at every stage (HJ9/SJ249 2025; HJ3/SJ3/SB311 2026) and did NOT cross over when ~15% of House Republicans did on HJ3 (final 67-31) -> affirms man-woman marriage. FAMILY: YES on SB778 (obscene material to a minor; penalty, 93-6). ELECTION: NO on absentee-ballot cure/extension expansions (HB773/HB774/HB82/HB968) and on the felon-voting-rights amendment HJ2. PUBLIC JUSTICE: YES on enhanced fentanyl/drug + assault-on-correctional-officer + sexual-extortion penalties; NO on bail-leniency, earned-sentence-credit, and parole-expansion bills. IMMIGRATION: YES on HB77 (state/local LE federal cooperation), NO on HB1441/SB783 (bills restricting local-federal immigration cooperation) -> opposes sanctuary limits. SELF-DEFENSE: voted NO on the entire gun-control slate both sessions (assault-weapons & magazine bans, red-flag/substantial-risk orders, safe-storage, waiting period, background-check expansions). No rubric-relevant cross-party deviations. Federal-overreach, CBDC/balanced-budget, and a dedicated photo-ID bill returned NO EVIDENCE this window and were left null.

candidates.name: Destiny LeVere Bolling
candidates.notes: VA Delegate HD-80 (Henrico), sitting since Jan 2024. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Progressive HARD record; VPAP party-unity 96-97% with no conservative-direction deviation (off-caucus votes are procedural/local — speed cameras, alcohol delivery, paid-sick-leave mechanics). Life: ReproRising 2025 = A+ 'Champion'; co-patron of the reproductive-freedom amendment; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia (2023). Marriage: same-sex-marriage amendment passed the House on party lines (HJR9 2025 58-35, full Democratic caucus in favor / HJ3 2026); as a 96-97%-unity Democrat she was in that majority. Election: 'waved a green flag in support of HJ2' (voting-rights amendment) Jan 2025 — recorded, but not mapped to the ballot-mechanics election-integrity cells. 2A: only a SOFT progressive-guide 'gun violence prevention' framing with no specific bill and no individual Giffords grade (Giffords scores states, not delegates) — left null, not inferred. Family/child, Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration, public justice: no isolated roll-call — left null. Baptist (biographical).

candidates.notes: VA Delegate HD-81 (Richmond area), sitting since 2009; Baptist associate minister at New Bridge Baptist Church. Life: co-patron HJ1 reproductive-freedom amendment; ReproRising 2025 = A+ 'Champion.' Marriage: named co-patron of HJ3 (2026)/HJ9 (2025) same-sex-marriage amendment. Economic: co-patron HB1 (2026) $15 minimum wage (recorded; does not map to the state economic_stewardship cells of CBDC/balanced-budget/usury/ESG, so left null). Election: co-patron HJ2 (2026) felon-voting-rights restoration (recorded, not mapped to ballot-mechanics cells). Only recent intra-party deviations are two 2024 public-safety/criminal-procedure bills (HB1583 firearm-threat penalties, HB2472 juvenile fingerprinting) — VPAP does not state direction, so not scored. 2A, family, Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration: no isolated directional roll-call — left null.

candidates.name: Kimberly Pope Adams
candidates.notes: VA Delegate HD-82 (Petersburg area), freshman sitting since Jan 2026; CPA/auditor, Baptist. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] STANDOUT MIXED RECORD with genuine documented cross-party credit. Contrary on social amendments: named co-patron of HJ1 (abortion-rights), HJ3 (same-sex marriage), and HJ2 (felon-voting restoration); endorsed by EMILY's List and Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia. BUT documented PRO-2A deviation: despite a Giffords endorsement she voted NAY on three 2026 gun-control bills — HB1524 and its companion SB727 ('assault firearms' public-carry ban) and HB871 (mandatory safe-storage) — siding with House Republicans against her own caucus on all three (primary LIS roll-calls). Public justice: campaign platform explicitly supports 'sufficient state funding for Sheriff and Police departments' (pro-police-funding statement). Family (VEA endorsement), economic (labor endorsements): SOFT only, no mapped vote — left null. Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration: thin freshman record, no evidence — left null. HJ2 felon-restoration co-patronage recorded but not mapped to the ballot-mechanics election-integrity cells.

candidates.name: Otto Wachsmann
candidates.notes: VA Delegate, HD-83 (R), sitting since 2022 (HD-75 then HD-83). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Genuinely conservative HARD voting record: consistent pro-life (voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment HJ1/SJ247 in BOTH 2025 and 2026 readings; ChoiceTracker rating 'Anti-Choice'; voted for born-alive protection). Strong 2A (NO on every Democratic gun-control bill -- assault-weapon/magazine bans, waiting periods, red-flag expansions, storage mandates, industry-liability). Election-integrity posture (NO on absentee/early-voting expansion, NO on automatic registration, NO on the felon-re-enfranchisement amendment HJ2). Anti-sanctuary on immigration (YES on HB77 authorizing local enforcement of federal immigration law; NO on HB1441/SB783 restricting local ICE cooperation). Tough-on-crime/pro-police (YES on Line-of-Duty-Act, retired-LEO protections; NO on marijuana sentence-leniency). KEY DEVIATION (verified roll calls): voted YES on the 2025 marriage-equality constitutional amendment (HJ9, 01/14/2025 -- one of only 7 GOP yes votes; and SJ249 companion) -- biblical_marriage q0 scored FALSE -- but REVERSED in 2026, voting NO on HJ3 2nd reference and all companion vehicles (SJ3, HB612, SB311) -- q1 scored TRUE with the reversal noted. Christian-liberty, school-choice/ESA, CBDC/sound-money, 10th-Amendment, E-Verify: no discrete recorded floor vote located -> left null, not inferred.

candidates.name: Virgil Thornton Sr.
candidates.notes: VA Delegate, HD-86 (D), FRESHMAN seated Jan 2026. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Thin record by design: scored from his own CITED public statements (interviews/campaign — labeled SOFT in research) plus one HARD chief-patron bill (HB773, 2026, expanding the absentee/provisional ballot-curing window; passed House 62-35). Stated public positions: backs the reproductive-freedom amendment and frames abortion as a decision for 'women, families, and their doctors' (endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates); names the marriage-equality amendment among his top-three priorities; 'strongly opposes diverting public funds to private or religious schools' (anti-school-choice); supports 'mandatory training and licensing for firearm ownership' (Giffords-endorsed). The four opening-day party-line amendment votes (HJ1/HJ2/HJ3) were NOT individually machine-confirmed, so they are NOT scored as his roll calls -- scoring rests on his explicit statements + HB773. candidates.name: Robert Bloxom Jr.
candidates.notes: VA House Delegate, HD-100 (R), since Feb 2014 — 12 years; rural Eastern Shore moderate-conservative. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] FLAGGED CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: (1) Voted YES on HJ9 (2025) and HJ3 (2026 re-passage) — same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment — one of only 7 named GOP crossovers in 2025 (Bloxom, Coyner, Garrett, Green, McNamara, Obenshain, Wachsmann); forum statement: 'supports protecting same-sex marriage.' (2) Publicly opposed a school voucher program at the 2025 ESVA Chamber forum. Held conservative line elsewhere where individually cited: pro-life ('Rob is pro-life,' forum quote that HJ1 amendment 'would enshrine partial birth abortions into law'); YEA on 2025 'Defend the Guard'; YEA on 2022 CRT ban + family-life-education transparency; FOP Lodge 8 endorsement; pro-2A campaign platform (Castle Doctrine, opposes 'any national gun registry'); NRA-PVF endorsed. Freedom Index lifetime 29% — moderate by GOP caucus standard. Won re-election Nov 4, 2025 over Liz Richardson 55-45.

profile.religion: United Methodist — active member, Grace United Methodist Church
profile.background: VA House of Delegates HD-100 (Eastern Shore — Accomack/Northampton + part of Virginia Beach) since Feb 2014. Owner Shore Tire & Auto and two NAPA stores in Onley/Mappsville plus Egret Pointe Seafood Co. Son of Del./VA Sec. of Agriculture Robert 'Bob' Bloxom Sr. (HD-100, 1978–2004).
profile.office: Room 711, General Assembly Building 201 N. 9th St., Richmond, VA 23219

candidates.name: Chris Yakabouski
candidates.notes: Longest-serving BOS member (since 2003). Self-identifies as far from moderate. Won 2025 re-election by only 16 votes vs. Braswell. Has clashed with local tea party/Patriots group over stadium and taxes. Runs Bob Yak and Sons Home Remodeling. Strong on 2A but has voted for tax increases, which draws conservative criticism. Pragmatic Republican — not a culture warrior.

profile.religion: Christian
profile.background: Conservative Spotsylvania supervisor, Battlefield District

claims.text: Strong on 2A but has voted for tax increases, which draws conservative criticism.

candidates.name: David Goosman
candidates.notes: STRONGEST CHRISTIAN CONSERVATIVE on the board. Baptist deacon who explicitly campaigns on Christian conservative principles. Retired Air Force officer, 21 years military service. Campaign website is goosmangop.org — the 'GOP' is in the URL. Defeated incumbent Kevin Marshall by a wide margin running as the more conservative option. His organization is explicitly 'committed to advancing Christian conservative leadership in Spotsylvania County.' Newly elected — limited voting record but strong faith-based platform.

candidates.name: Gerald Childress
candidates.notes: Marine veteran and Spotsylvania County native. Graduated from Spotsylvania High School. Served on Planning Commission (Chairman), Transportation Committee, and Economic Development Authority before BOS. Explicitly states belief in 'limited government, individual responsibility, and fiscal conservatism.' Initially pro-data-center development but shifted to support SUP requirement. First term — building voting record.

candidates.name: Drew Mullins
candidates.office: Board of Supervisors — Courtland District (Vice Chairman)
candidates.notes: Most decorated military veteran on the board — 23-year Navy SEAL with 14+ deployments. Elected Vice Chairman in Jan 2026 (Frazier was perturbed by the vote). Campaign focuses on economic development, competitive county employee salaries, and public safety — NOT culture war issues. Pragmatic Republican who emphasizes 'healthy county growth.' Small business owner (Azimuth Analytics). UMW graduate pursuing graduate degree at University of Arizona.

candidates.name: Lori Hayes
candidates.office: Board of Supervisors — Lee Hill District (Chairman)
candidates.notes: Board Chairman and independent who caucuses with Republicans. Social work degree from GMU. Co-owned Calypso Kennels in Stafford with her brother (who died in 2023). Strongest 2A score due to proactive leadership on sanctuary reaffirmation. Runs as independent — NOT endorsed through GOP nomination. Supported redistricting referendum opposition (6-0). Pragmatic, growth-management focused. Limited public positions on social/cultural issues — scores lower on life, marriage, and christian_nationalism due to absence of evidence, not contrary evidence.

profile.religion: Christian
profile.background: Chairman, Spotsylvania Board of Supervisors, Lee Hill District

candidates.name: Jacob Lane
candidates.notes: Blue-collar Republican — Luck Stone foreman who has been involved in county issues since his 20s. Won Feb 2022 special election after Barry Jett's death. Claims ideological alignment with Jett. Vocally defended the board's redistricting referendum resolution saying it was about 'equal and fair' timing for both sides. One of the most vocal 2A supporters — brought it up proactively during the Jan 2026 meeting. Youngest BOS member at ~40. Infrastructure and growth management focused.

candidates.name: Dr. Deborah H. Frazier
candidates.notes: LONE DEMOCRAT on the board. First African American BOS member in Spotsylvania history. Career educator (25-year principal, now Chief Academic Officer). Abstained from collective bargaining opposition letter (6-0, she abstained) saying she was 'on the fence.' Abstained from redistricting referendum opposition (6-0, she abstained). Was 'perturbed' when Mullins was elected Vice Chair over her or her preferred candidate in Jan 2026. Served on Spanberger's gubernatorial transition team. Notable: she voted YES on 2A sanctuary BOTH times — suggesting some independence from Democratic Party on gun issues in this rural-suburban county. Scored 2 on self_defense because she did support 2A sanctuary. Education score reflects career dedication but from progressive establishment perspective, not parental rights/school choice angle.

claims.text: Notable: she voted YES on 2A sanctuary BOTH times — suggesting some independence from Democratic Party on gun issues in this rural-suburban county.

candidates.name: Kerry Devine
candidates.notes:
Mayor since 2024. Registered Independent but Dem-endorsed. Council under her leadership: LGBTQIA+ Pride Month, DEI commitment, 100% renewable energy resolution. No public stances on abortion, marriage, immigration, or religious liberty. Supports gun giveback program.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor, At-Large, term 2024-2027 (fredericksburgva.gov/263). HARD evidence: voted for FY2027 budget raising the real-estate tax rate 4 cents to $0.84/$100 and a two-tier water/sewer rate hike, and personally pushed to advertise a rate 1 cent higher 'for flexibility' (economic_stewardship FALSE). Publicly supported the city Gun Give-Back and backed Gov. Northam's proposal giving localities more authority to regulate firearms (self_defense FALSE). As mayor, the city officially observes LGBTQIA+ Pride Month, flies Pride flags downtown, and maintains a Commitment to Equity & Inclusion (biblical_marriage q4 FALSE, institutional). The FY2027 budget she presided over funds a public-safety pay plan with no defunding — counted as routine governance, not the affirmative public advocacy public_justice q0 requires, so left null. No church/faith affiliation found in two long-form interviews or her official bio. NO EVIDENCE found on abortion, schools curriculum, election administration, or immigration at the council level — those cells are null, not penalized.

profile.education: University of Mary Washington
profile.background: Social studies teacher, Stafford County. City Council since 2004. School Board 1996-2004. Mother of four.

candidates.name: Charlie Frye Jr.
candidates.notes:
Most documented progressive on council. Teamster (Local 322), 24-yr UPS driver. Created gun giveback program (200+ guns collected since 2014). Led slave auction block removal. 100% renewable energy by 2035. Progressive Voters Guide "most progressive choice." Member Shiloh Baptist Church but policy positions entirely progressive.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Vice Mayor, Ward 4, re-elected/named VM Jan 2026, term 2026-2029. HARD evidence: founded the Fredericksburg Gun Give-Back in 2014 (242 firearms collected by Jan 2026), championed statewide HB702 modeled on it, and backed expanding local firearm-regulation authority (self_defense FALSE). Voted for two consecutive 4-cent real-estate tax-rate increases — FY2026 to $0.80 and FY2027 to $0.84/$100 (economic_stewardship FALSE). Public safety his stated No. 1 priority; runs police-partnership programs ('Murder Free Fredericksburg') and credited the city with zero homicides in 2025 (public_justice TRUE). Prior speculative party-default FALSEs on abortion/marriage/elections cleared to null — NO direct evidence of personal positions on those was found.

profile.background: Vice Mayor since 2020. Ward 4 Councilman since 2014. Only Black member of council. Gun give-back program. Racial equity advocate.

candidates.name: Joshua Cole
candidates.notes: VA Delegate, HD-65 (Fredericksburg area); Baptist pastor; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record and an active LEADER of the measures most opposed by this rubric: CHIEF SPONSOR of the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJR1; A+/'Champion' from ReproRising) and CHIEF SPONSOR of the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3; he is himself bisexual). Led automatic voter registration + felon-voting-rights restoration. Gun-control endorsed (Giffords: voted for safe-storage + universal background checks). Supported pro-transgender school policy; opposes book 'bans.' No conservative deviations found. Confirms the prior 0/70 read. (Note: this record was de-duplicated 2026-06 — a second 'joshua-g-cole' HD-65 record was merged in.)

Voted for the Bipartisan Safer Communities Act (June 2022) and issued a joint statement celebrating its passage; the law creates financial incentives for state red-flag laws, expands juvenile background-check requirements, and tightens straw-purchase prohibitions — directly opposing the rubric's rejection of red-flag laws.

Championed the DREAM Act and backed the 2024 bipartisan Senate border deal, which offered humanitarian parole authority and legal-residency pathways rather than the mandatory-deportation framework the rubric requires.

profile.religion: Evangelical Christian — founding member of East End Fellowship, a multi-ethnic evangelical church in Church Hill, Richmond (NOT Catholic). Public bio at https://www.theologicalhorizons.org/events1/dannyavula confirms. Richmonder.org (campaign) flagged his 'Christian faith and affiliation with a church that's taken conservative stances on marriage and abortion' as a campaign issue — which Avula countered by publicly affirming LGBTQ equality and abortion rights.
profile.education: B.A. Biology, University of Virginia (1999); M.D., Virginia Commonwealth University; M.P.H., Johns Hopkins.
profile.background: Born 1978 in Hyderabad, India; immigrated as a child — first Asian-American and first immigrant Mayor of Richmond. Pediatrician by training. Career: Director, Richmond/Henrico Health Districts (6 yrs); Virginia State Vaccination Coordinator (Jan 2021 – early 2022, appointed by Gov. Northam); Commissioner, Virginia Department of Social Services (under Northam and Youngkin). Sworn in as 81st Mayor of Richmond Jan 1 2025; current term ends Jan 1 2029. Wife Mary Kay (RPS elementary teacher); five children including one adopted; openly gay daughter (featured in 2024 campaign ad). Self-described 'committed Democrat' on an officially-nonpartisan Richmond mayoral ballot.

candidates.name: Andrew Breton
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 1 (West End), 'Gumby' Breton, first term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028. Former chair of Richmond City Democratic Committee — real-world Democratic alignment on an officially-nonpartisan ballot. RPS public-school dad / former Mary Munford PTA fundraising chair (institutional ally of the public-school system). Most of his public posture is technocratic-progressive-urbanist (walkability, zoning reform / 'missing middle,' multimodal transit, RVAgreen 2050 climate plan, gas-utility phase-out, gun-safety) with DELIBERATE silence on culture-war markers (abortion, marriage, parental rights, religion). Per the SKILL's 'NO EVIDENCE FOUND beats inference' discipline, the absence of cited quotes on those markers is captured as null rather than inferred FALSE. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: Richmond Mayorathon candidate Q&A — 'Funding resources and interventions for those who are most at-risk to commit violence will reduce incidents' is textbook public-health / intervention framing of violent crime; direction-adverse on q3. HARD FALSE self_defense q0 + q1: organized the June 7 2023 children's march on the Virginia State Capitol for gun-safety legislation following the Huguenot HS graduation shooting (bretonforrichmond.com/issues; Daily Progress 6/8/2023). Moms Demand Action is listed as a campaign endorser (bretonforrichmond.com/endorsements). Multi-vector pro-gun-control posture is direction-adverse to the constitutional-carry / oppose-restrictions cells. Sept 8 2025 vote: supported activating the Civilian Review Board for RPD oversight, quote 'I'm glad to see that we can get the process started… many Richmonders will be glad to see that we're adding this additional layer of fair accountability to our justice system'; also said 'I believe it's very appropriate that we're also taking this moment to affirm Richmond's commitment to good policing now and moving forward' (WTVR 9/8/2025). NOT scored TRUE on public_justice q0 — CRB activation is a process / accountability measure, not affirmative pro-police advocacy, and Breton's 'good policing' language is supportive-of-accountability framing not affirmative thin-blue-line; doesn't clear the HIGH BAR. NOT scored FALSE on q0 either — he didn't vote to defund and his 'commitment to good policing' framing is supportive enough to leave null per the strict cite-only discipline. NOT scored economic_stewardship q2 — supports a rebate / tax-relief approach ('we have room to roll back the tax rate' at richmonder.org candidate forum), but his specific yea/nay on the May 12-13 2025 FY26 budget 6-2 vote was not confirmed in retrieved sources; also backed an independent salary study for city employees and is mostly silent on aggressive rate cuts; left null per cite-only discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / public_justice q1/q2/q4 / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration — no quote-bearing positions located despite extensive search of campaign site, city council records, candidate questionnaires, and news coverage. Sparse 3 FALSE / 0 TRUE on cited cells reflects his deliberate culture-war silence in public materials — honest report per the 'NO EVIDENCE FOUND beats inference' brand discipline. Counter-archetype context: civic-review-board support + public-health framing of crime + Moms Demand endorsement establish a clear progressive-urbanist coalition profile, but the absence of any pro-life, pro-traditional-marriage, pro-school-choice, or pro-religious-liberty quotes also documents the deliberate silence — neither inference is fair. Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget vote, the June 10 2026 Pride proclamation cycle (will he sign or co-sponsor anything?), and any 2026 council action on sanctuary, abortion, or firearms.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of denominational affiliation or church membership. No public statement of faith in campaign materials, council bios, or news coverage. Wedding was at the Virginia Museum of Fine Arts — civil, not ecclesiastical — suggestive but not dispositive.
profile.education: B.A. University of Virginia (2004); graduate degree in economics, Duke University (2012).
profile.background: Born ~1982 in the D.C. area. Career: financial sector at BlackRock in New York City; left after the 2008 crisis ('witnessed banks bailed out while working families lost everything'); moved to Richmond 2008. Currently a financial data architect / data engineer. Wife is a physician (VCU Medical School); at least one child at Mary Munford Elementary (Breton served as PTA board member and fundraising chair). Prior civic work in Boston with Progressive Massachusetts (chapter chair, statewide board). Former CHAIR of the Richmond City Democratic Committee before running for council. Organized the June 7 2023 children's march on the Virginia State Capitol for gun-safety legislation following the Huguenot HS graduation shooting. Richmond City Council, District 1 (West End), officially-nonpartisan ballot, sworn 1/1/2025; term 1/1/2025 – 12/31/2028. Won Nov 5 2024 general 49.1% defeating Paul Goldman 27.7% and Zachary Walker 22.4%, succeeding Andreas Addison (who ran for mayor).

EDUCATION: UVa Urban & Environmental Planning grad degree. HARD FALSE sanctity_of_life q1: voluntarily asked to be ADDED as a patron of the July 2024 ordinance unanimously approving transfer of the former Brook Hill School site to the Virginia League for Planned Parenthood for a $6M health center (richmondbizsense.com 7/23/2024); joined patrons Addison, Lambert, Jones, Nye, Lynch, and Stoney. Explicit affirmative facilitation of a PP clinic land-transfer on city-controlled property — rubric-adverse direction on q1 ('refused to declare sanctuary city for abortion' / pro-life city resolutions). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4: signed the June 10 2025 'Joint Proclamation: Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community' with Mayor Avula and Council President Newbille, declaring Richmond an 'official Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community, in perpetuity' and a 'safe place for LGBTQ+ people to live their lives freely, openly, authentically' — signed in her capacity as Council Vice President (rva.gov 6/10/2025). Affirmative Pride proclamation, the cleanest hit on q4 ('opposes city/county Pride proclamations' inverse). HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: voted NO on the Nov 12 2024 proposed real estate tax rate reduction from $1.20 to $1.16 per $100 (vpm.org 11/12/2024 + richmondfreepress.com 11/14/2024); in 2025 backed Avula's plan to keep the rate the same when the cut was proposed again; as VP whipped the council on the FY26 $3B budget with the 'We are in this together as a city' line (richmondbizsense.com 5/13/2025). Three-vector vote-against-restraint posture — rubric-adverse on q2. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: 2020 Richmond Magazine Q&A framed gun violence and homelessness around 'upstream issues' like living wages and wraparound services rather than emergency responses; supports a social-worker mental-health-call response model that displaces police on mental-health calls — explicit root-causes / public-health framing of violent crime (rubric-adverse on q3). NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR not cleared): she stated 'broader public safety approach that includes social workers for mental health and substance abuse calls' and 'All departments, including police, must justify funding requests during fiscal constraints' (Richmond Magazine 2020) and supports civilian review board with enforcement power — these are governance-neutral or LE-skeptical, NOT affirmative pro-police defense, so null per discipline rather than FALSE (which would require an affirmative anti-LE vote that didn't surface). NOT scored christian_liberty q3: the 2021 restoration of tax-exempt status to Community Church of God in Christ (richmondfreepress.com 7/1/2021) could be relevant but no individually-attributed Jordan vote / quote located — null per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0 / biblical_marriage q0+q2 / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty other cells / election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense / industry_capture / public_justice q1+q2+q4 — no quote-bearing positions located. ADDITIONAL CONTEXTUAL MARKERS: sponsored Richmond's Climate and Ecological Emergency Resolution July 2021 (commonwealthtimes.org 9/29/2021) — ESG-direction signal but no specific local rubric cell maps cleanly; lone NO on BOTH Richmond casino referendums with quote 'I realize I'm the outlier. That's not a role I like, but I think we all have to vote our conscience' (richmonder.org) — principled-dissent flag but no rubric cell maps. Endorsed by Sierra Club, Home Builders Association, Richmond Association of Realtors, and Rep. Jennifer McClellan (D-VA-04) in 2024. 0 TRUE / 4 FALSE on cited cells = 0% — coherent progressive-D council vice president with PP facilitation + Pride proclamation signatory + anti-tax-cut + public-health-crime framing. Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget cycle.

candidates.name: Kenya Gibson
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 3 (Northside), sworn 1/1/2025, term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Prior service on Richmond Public Schools School Board District 3 (2018-2024). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed. EDUCATION: William & Mary B.A. RICH DUAL-TENURE RECORD: prior RPS SB service (2018-2024) adds scoreable family_child_sovereignty + biblical_marriage cells beyond a typical first-term council member. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4: as sitting councilmember Gibson co-presented Richmond's 2026 Pride Month Proclamation with Mayor Avula at the May 29 2026 City Hall Pride flag-raising ceremony (wvtf.org 5/29/2026 + richmonder.org). Affirmative Pride proclamation participation (rubric-adverse on q4). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q2: as Richmond School Board D3 member Gibson was one of 8 board members to vote YES on the Oct 3 2022 resolution REJECTING Gov. Youngkin's model transgender student policies and affirming protections for students 'regardless of sexual orientation, gender identity or gender expression' (newsweek 10/3/2022 + rvahub 10/4/2022); vote was 8-1, only Jonathan Young dissented, Gibson among the 8 affirmatives — the rubric-adverse direction on q2 ('opposes city/county trans-affirming policies' inverse). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: same Oct 2022 RPS vote maps directly to the local family q2 cell ('school board has voted to REMOVE CRT/SOGI/comprehensive-sex-ed/gender-ideology materials' inverse) — she voted to RETAIN/AFFIRM the trans-affirming gender-identity framework. HARD FALSE public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR cleared in REVERSE direction): as RPS SB member Sept 2021, Gibson introduced the MOTION to end RPS's MOU with Richmond Police and remove School Resource Officers by June 2023, replacing them with 'community-sourced safety professionals' + restorative-justice task force (vpm.org 10/5/2021 + wric.com); motion failed 3-6 but she SPONSORED it. RVA Mag op-ed corroborates 'she argued for the removal of police from schools.' As current councilmember she also opposes the Flock license-plate-reader contract (vpm.org 5/11/2026 + 5/20/2026). Multi-vector affirmative anti-LE advocacy — rubric-adverse on q0. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: campaign site quote in her own voice — gun violence is 'baked into our society through extreme economic inequality and racist policy' requiring anti-poverty spending and gun restrictions; cites firearm deaths as 'leading cause of mortality for children and teens'; earned Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' distinction (Aug 29 2024, kenyagibson.com updates). Explicit public-health / structural-inequality framing of violent crime (rubric-adverse on q3). HARD FALSE self_defense q1: Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' distinction Aug 29 2024 for commitments on gun restrictions and gun control at state/federal level — direct gun-control endorsement on the rubric-adverse direction. NOT scored economic_stewardship q2: ABSTAINED on FY26 real estate tax rate Sept 2025 asking for more data on collective-bargaining costs (vpm.org 9/23/2025); did not affirmatively push to cut or hold the rate — null per discipline. RVA Mag op-ed states Gibson 'pushed for progressive taxation — raising taxes on the wealthy' but no specific sponsored Richmond-council tax-increase ordinance located, so the FALSE-direction signal is suggestive not vote-bearing. NOT scored border_immigration q1/q2: Richmond as a body has long-standing 1990 sanctuary ordinance and Mayor Avula publicly opposes 287(g) cooperation, but no individually-attributed Gibson sponsored-vote on sanctuary status located — null per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / family_child_sovereignty q0+q1 / christian_liberty / election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach / industry_capture / public_justice q1+q2+q4 / self_defense q0 — no quote-bearing positions located despite the dual-tenure record. ENDORSEMENT COALITION: NVM, SEIU VA State Council (incl. 32BJ + 512), LiUNA Local 804, Richmond Education Association, Teamsters Local 322, Sunrise Movement Richmond, Richmond DSA, Richmond for All, The Collective PAC, Lead Locally (2024 Green New Deal Slate), Moms Demand Action — uniformly progressive-D coalition. 0 TRUE / 6 FALSE on cited cells = 0% — coherent progressive-D council member with rich RPS SB history backing the school-board family/marriage cells (Oct 2022 trans resolution + Sept 2021 SRO removal motion). Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget cycle and any 2027 cultural-issue council votes.

candidates.name: Ellen Robertson
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 6 (Gateway), serving continuously since Jan 2004 (sworn after Nov 2003 election); re-elected Nov 5 2024 53.3% three-way race; current term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. RELIGION: not publicly disclosed. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: voted YES on Nov 12 2024 Trammell-proposed real estate tax rate cut from $1.20 to $1.16 per $100 (failed 5-4; Robertson with Trammell/Lambert/Lynch in the YES minority); actively pressured the Avula administration in committee for a lower-rate baseline budget — quoted asking CAO Odie Donald II whether the administration would build the budget around a lower rate if Council asked; Donald replied 'No ma'am' and Robertson pushed back suggesting $16M in prior-year surplus could absorb the $3.2M revenue hit. Heterodox-D fiscal-restraint signal — the cleanest cross-party catch this batch (parallel to Trammell/Lambert/Lynch faction against the Newbille/Avula hold-the-rate coalition). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q2: Mayor Levar Stoney publicly credited Robertson (with then-Councilmember Parker Agelasto) as 'key in moving Richmond's Human Rights Commission and non-discrimination laws forward in the city council,' which produced the city's SOGI non-discrimination framework AND 'transgender-inclusive health benefits for city employees' that lifted Richmond's HRC Municipal Equality Index score 52 points (chpn.net 2018) — affirmative advocacy on the trans-affirming-policy-for-city-employees direction, the rubric's adverse direction on q2. HARD FALSE public_justice q1: voted with then-Councilmember Mike Jones in committee 2-1 to advance the 2020 police-defunding study resolution to full council with a recommendation to approve (wric.com); founded Richmond's Office of Community Wealth Building (root-causes-of-poverty framing); chairs the city's Alternatives to Incarceration Board; on the Northam marijuana-legalization-amendments advance she framed legalization as supporting 'people who are incarcerated for possession' and 'help[ing] people seek health assistance' (wric.com) — explicit decarceral / soft-on-crime direction across multiple vectors. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: in her own voice as Council Vice President, May 13 2021 gun-violence-prevention rally — 'Gun violence is certainly a public crisis that extends beyond Richmond; it's a national crisis that calls for us to use all the resources at our disposal to keep people safe' (rva.gov) — textbook public-health/national-crisis framing of violent crime, the rubric-adverse direction on q3 (parallel to Doyle 'rec centers / libraries / community groups' framing scored FALSE in the 1550 Norfolk batch). NOT scored biblical_marriage q4 (Pride proclamations): research confirms Robertson was NOT a signatory on the June 10 2025 Joint Proclamation: Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community (Avula + Newbille + Jordan signed; Robertson did not) — null not FALSE per strict cite-only discipline (absence of signature is not affirmative opposition; she also has not publicly dissented). NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR not cleared): Robertson said 'Richmond needs a police department. I am open to exploring concerns and addressing them' on the 2020 defund proposal but did not make an affirmative pro-police defense of the chief or oppose-defund advocacy; her actual committee vote ADVANCED the defund study. Null on q0 + the FALSE on q1 captures the soft-on-crime posture without overreach. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense / industry_capture / public_justice q2 + q4 — no quote-bearing positions located in 22 years of service despite the long council tenure (sparse cultural-issue record on a fiscally-mixed urban progressive). 1 TRUE / 3 FALSE on cited cells = 25% — coherent center-left urban D council member whose distinctive feature is heterodox fiscal restraint (Trammell-coalition tax-cut yes-vote + active administration pressure for a lower-rate budget). Recommend revisiting if Robertson signs a future Pride proclamation, takes a position on the 2025 Avula sanctuary stance, or after the FY27 budget cycle.

candidates.name: Cynthia Newbille
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 7 (East End), current term 1/2/2025-12/31/2028, currently Council President 2025-2026 (3rd stint). Officially nonpartisan; real-world Democratic alignment. HARD TRUE public_justice q0 (CLEARS HIGH BAR via multi-vector affirmative pro-police record): (1) July 2020 amid George Floyd unrest she voted with majority REJECTING reductions to the RPD budget in response to defund-the-police demands (Richmond Free Press 7/30/2020, WRIC) — 'conditional no' on defund framework; (2) 2022 affirmatively proposed $1.9M-$4M in RPD and FIRE pay raises with VP Robertson and Trammell (multi-source) — that is advocacy, not routine governance; (3) Feb 2026 walked Shockoe Bottom businesses with Police Chief Rick Edwards after a mass shooting, quoted 'The goal is to share, to hear from the community, and to really map out strategies' — partnership/community-policing work beyond budget. Three independent vectors of affirmative pro-police advocacy clears the SKILL's HIGH BAR ('NOT just routine collaboration… affirmative advocacy: runs a police-partnership program, public pro-police statements'). This is a meaningful counter-archetype catch — a Democratic-aligned council president with a real pro-police record. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4: as Council President co-signed the June 10 2025 Joint Proclamation 'Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community in perpetuity' with Mayor Avula and Council VP Jordan (rva.gov). Affirmative state-government LGBTQ promotion = direction-adverse. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: Oct 14-15 2025 co-sponsored with Mayor Avula the proposal to HOLD the real-estate tax rate steady at $1.20/$100, defeating Reva Trammell's proposed cut; direct Newbille quote: 'I look forward to when we will be able to reduce the tax rate. I do not see that in this moment.' (VPM 10/15/2025). Direct on-record opposition to tax-rate cut → adverse on rate-restraint cell. Voted YES on May 13 2025 6-2 adoption of the $3B FY26 budget keeping the $1.20 rate. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: May 24 2021 voted with unanimous council to declare gun violence a 'public health crisis' alongside Mayor Stoney (WTVR 5/13/2021, WRIC, RVA.gov GVPI page). Given her public-health PhD and decades of public-health-policy career, this is the most quintessential q3 fit on the council. HARD FALSE refuse_state_overreach q2: Sept 13 2021 voted with unanimous council to pass the Climate and Ecological Emergency Resolution and endorsed creation of a residential energy-efficiency program (Sierra Club Falls of the James, Richmonder energy-efficiency story) — the rubric's q2 cell specifically names ESG, and unanimous-council ESG-aligned action with her vote = direction-adverse. HARD FALSE border_immigration q2: as Council President co-signed admin statement 'Local government is not in charge of federal immigration law enforcement and Richmond does not detain or arrest anyone due to immigration status' (RVA.gov immigration response page); RPD under city policy has not signed a 287(g) agreement; Richmond appears/appeared on the federal sanctuary-jurisdictions list; in July 2025 when a federal agent accessed RPD Flock Safety camera data for immigration enforcement Newbille stayed publicly silent despite multiple media inquiries (Church Hill Lookout — HARD documentation of non-response). Anti-ICE-cooperation posture = adverse on q2's federal-preemption-against-sanctuary cell. HARD FALSE self_defense q0 + q1: July 1 2019 voted YES on the ordinance banning firearms in Richmond city buildings and parks; direct Newbille quote characterized it as 'one way to limit gun violence' and said council 'needed to act to help citizens feel safe on city property' (WTVR 7/1/2019); supported (with delay for refinement) Stoney's ordinance prohibiting firearms near permitted events. Richmond did NOT pass a Second Amendment Sanctuary resolution under her council presidency, in contrast to most VA rural localities in the 2019-2020 wave — directionally adverse to the constitutional-carry / oppose-restrictions cells. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / public_justice q1/q2/q4 / refuse_state_overreach q0/q1/q3/q4 / border_immigration q1 — no quote-bearing positions located. NOT scored biblical_marriage q0/q2 — Pride proclamation cite scores q4 (LGBTQ promotion) directly but no marriage-definition or biological-sex quote → null per strict cite-only discipline. Counter-archetype context (CAPTURED in q0 TRUE): she is NOT a defunder. Her July 2020 vote with the majority rejecting police-budget cuts + her 2022 pay-raise proposal + her partnership work with Chief Edwards are real and meaningful softeners on the standard Democratic-progressive coding. Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget vote and any 2026 ICE-policy or 2A council action.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of specific congregation or denominational affiliation. Richmond Magazine 'Flip to the Back' profile records personal piety quotes ('When all else fails, I pray, I plan, I proceed' and reads religious literature) — SOFT evidence of personal Christian practice without a publicly-identifiable church home.
profile.education: B.A. psychology/linguistics, SUNY Stony Brook (1974); M.A. psychology, SUNY Stony Brook (1983); Ph.D. Public Policy/Administration with Health Policy concentration, Virginia Commonwealth University (2010).
profile.background: Raised in Whitcomb Court public housing in the East End; Whitcomb Elementary, Mosby Middle, Armstrong High School. Public-health / nonprofit career: Executive Director, National Black Women's Health Project (Atlanta, early 1990s); Head Start Program director at Charles Drew University (Compton, CA); Interim Co-director, Richmond City Public Health Department; Associate Director, VCU Grace E. Harris Leadership Institute; Senior Advisor, Richmond Memorial Health Foundation; Annie E. Casey Foundation consultant; Chief of Staff to Richmond City Manager. Co-founder of SisterFund (2015), giving circle for African-American women/girls. Sports Backers board member. Vegetarian. Lives near 27th & Nine Mile Road, East End. Richmond City Council, District 7 (East End), officially-nonpartisan (real-world Democratic alignment). First elected via special election 2009; current term Jan 2 2025 – Dec 31 2028 (re-elected Nov 5 2024 with 66.46% over Eric Sundberg's 32.83%). candidates.name: Reva Trammell
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 8 (Southside), in office since Jan 2007 (multi-decade tenure); re-elected Nov 5 2024 over Frank Wilson Jr.; current term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. RELIGION: not publicly disclosed. HETERODOX-D CATCH (the biggest find this batch): the ONLY Richmond councilmember who refused to co-sponsor the 2022 post-Dobbs abortion-protection resolution — every other member + Mayor Stoney signed, Trammell did not (vpm.org 2024-07-23 Planned Parenthood land-transfer context). HARD TRUE sanctity_of_life q1: that 2022 refusal is the cleanest cross-party signal on the rubric's sanctuary-city-for-abortion inverse direction. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: most aggressive tax-cutter on Council — repeatedly proposed 4-cent rate cuts from $1.20 to $1.16 in 2022, 2024, and 2025; in Oct 2025 she was one of only 3 YES votes for the cut with Abubaker and Lynch (vpm.org 2025-10-15 + richmonder.org). Multi-cycle restraint signal that crosses the Avula-administration / Newbille leadership line. HARD TRUE public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR cleared, AFFIRMATIVE): July 2020 vote against police budget reassignment analysis — 'Our police officers, I mean it breaks my heart to see what is going on... they are being belittled, how they are being cussed out, how they're being told F the police' (wric.com). Co-proposed 2022 police+firefighter pay raises ($1.9-$4M) with Newbille and Robertson. Chair of Public Safety Committee. RPD publicly credited her for the Jeff Davis corridor cleanup. THIS IS THE HIGH-BAR cell that requires affirmative advocacy — Trammell clearly clears it (parallel to but stronger than the federal pro-police caucus signal). HARD TRUE public_justice q1: voted NO on the 2020 RPD-budget-reassignment-to-mental-health-services analysis — a defund-adjacent measure; voted to strike the non-lethal weapon ban in 2020 committee (richmondfreepress.com 2020-07-30 + vpm.org 2020-09-29). HARD TRUE self_defense q0: abstained on the 2019 Richmond ordinance banning guns on city property/parks, stating the ordinance 'would not prevent those intent on doing harm from doing so' and objecting to process (wtvr.com 2019-07-01); voted to STRIKE a 2020 non-lethal weapon ban in committee (vpm.org 2020-09-29). Strike-vote is the affirmative action that clears the rubric on q0. NOT scored biblical_marriage cells: no public Trammell-individual record on Pride proclamations (Richmond's joint proclamations have been Mayor + Council Pres + VP signatories, not full council), no public position on drag-in-libraries / trans / SOGI located — null per discipline. NOT scored christian_liberty cells: no public Trammell-individual record. NOT scored election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration: no quote-bearing Trammell-individual votes / statements surfaced. CONFEDERATE MONUMENTS NOTE (not scored — no rubric cell): initially voted NO three times before reversing to support removal — populist constituent-driven not ideological. 5 TRUE / 0 FALSE on cited cells across sanctity_of_life (1), economic_stewardship (1), public_justice (2), self_defense (1) — a heterodox-D council member with a coherent populist/pro-cop/tax-cutter brand that genuinely deviates from the Richmond progressive machine on multiple vectors. This is the cleanest cross-party catch in the Richmond batches so far.
