candidates.name: Nicole Jones
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council South Central 9th Voter District, appointed interim 12/12/2023 (sworn 1/1/2024), elected to full term Nov 5 2024 w/ 58%, current term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Prior service on RPS School Board D9 (2021-2023). Fix party Unknown→I (Richmond CC races are officially nonpartisan per VA code; no formal D/R registration confirmed though coalition is uniformly D-aligned). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed. CAREER: ART 180 nonprofit Deputy Director. REAL-WORLD ALIGNMENT: D-coalition — endorsed by New Virginia Majority, SEIU, state Sen. Lamont Bagby (D), multiple RPS School Board members, predecessor Dr. Michael J. Jones (D, now HD-77), and listed in 2024 Progressive Voters Guide; also endorsed by Richmond Association of Realtors, Richmond Building Trades, Home Building Association of Richmond. No GOP or single-issue conservative endorsements. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: voted NO on the Oct 14 2025 proposal to cut Richmond real estate tax rate from $1.20 to $1.16 per $100 (failed 3-5-1; Jones in the 5-vote NO majority that defeated the cut). Quoted on the vote: 'We don't have nothing to show that by changing this rate or the rate that we keep is actually going to get us to where we need to be' (12onyourside.com 10/15/2025 + vpm.org 10/15/2025). Direct vote-against-restraint posture — rubric-adverse on q2 (Norfolk-batch precedent maps local q2 to rate restraint). Aligned with Newbille/Avula coalition, NOT the Trammell/Lambert/Lynch fiscal-pushback faction. SPARSE-DATA OUTCOME: ALL 29 OTHER LOCAL CELLS NULL per strict cite-only discipline. Only ~17 mo on council (~24 mo counting interim service); narrow policy lane (housing, basic city services, restorative justice framing); has not surfaced quote-bearing positions on culture-war markers. NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR): no affirmative pro-police quote located; she opposes Flock LPR contract alignment with Gibson (vpm.org 5/11/2026). NOT scored biblical_marriage q4: Richmond issued a joint Mayor/Council LGBTQ+ Friendly Community proclamation June 10 2025 but the document was signed by Avula + Newbille + Jordan, not Jones individually — null per discipline (absence of signature is not affirmative opposition). NOT scored border_immigration q1/q2: Richmond as a body has 1990 sanctuary ordinance + Mayor Avula publicly opposes 287(g); no individually-attributed Jones sponsored-vote located. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / family_child_sovereignty (despite SB service 2021-2023, no specific votes attributable surfaced in this batch) / christian_liberty / election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach / industry_capture / public_justice q2-q4 / self_defense — no quote-bearing positions located. 0 TRUE / 1 FALSE on cited cells = 0% — coherent first-term D-coalition councilmember whose only scoreable position so far is the Oct 2025 vote-against-rate-cut (clean Newbille/Avula coalition placement). Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget cycle and any 2027 cultural-issue or sanctuary council action.

candidates.name: Colette McEachin
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Commonwealth's Attorney, City of Richmond, VA (D); new term seated Jan 2026 after winning 2025 Dem primary 71% and running unopposed Nov 2025 general. EDUCATION CORRECTION: Brown undergrad + UVA Law (not W&M / Anglican as I speculated). RELIGION CORRECTION: institutionally Baptist (St. Peter Baptist CDC board), NOT AME (her late husband was the AME minister, not her). SOROS-DA FINDING: NO evidence she received Soros / Justice & Public Safety PAC money — that money flowed to her 2025 challenger Tom Barbour. She publicly declined to join VA Progressive Prosecutors for Justice and was attacked from her left in the primary. Heritage Foundation / LELDF Soros-DA rosters do NOT list her. HARD TRUE election_integrity q4: Oct 6 2025 her office personally announced a 4-count Class-5 felony election-fraud indictment of 2024 Richmond City Council candidate Tavares Floyd after a 10-month Virginia State Police probe (campaign-finance falsification, dead 'donors') — affirmative willingness to prosecute election crime. HARD FALSE public_justice q1: continued Herring's policy of not seeking cash bail in many cases — 'virtually eliminated the cash bond system for those awaiting trial' (Virginia Mercury); diversion programs for first-time non-violent, addiction, mental-health, behavioral-health; created a 'Community Justice Reform Unit to tackle root causes of crime'; expanded expungement. Multi-vector bail-reform / soft-on-crime trigger per the rubric definition (even though she also prosecutes murders aggressively and explicitly declined the 'progressive prosecutor' label — net cited direction is FALSE on q1). HARD FALSE public_justice q3: same 'Community Justice Reform Unit to tackle root causes of crime' + Behavioral Health Docket + restorative-justice integration = public-health framing of crime (rubric-adverse). HARD FALSE border_immigration q1 + q2: office statement 'We do not work with any local, state or federal law enforcement agency to detain or arrest anyone based solely upon their immigration status'; McEachin direct quote 'ICE cannot just barge into the Richmond Circuit Court courthouse, or any of the courthouses, and just grab people'; personally distributes 'blue cards' to immigrant communities in English/Spanish explaining rights to refuse searches and access counsel; office denied FOIA request for ICE communications; her quote 'This is me saying to Trump, the Constitution still lives.' Clear anti-ICE-cooperation posture on the federal-preemption-against-sanctuary cell. profile.background: First woman and first African-American woman elected Commonwealth's Attorney for the City of Richmond. 25+ years in the Richmond CA's office before winning the 2019 special general election after predecessor Mike Herring resigned to join McGuireWoods (May 2019). Re-elected unopposed in 2021. Won the June 17 2025 Democratic primary over Tom Barbour Jr. (~71% vs ~29%) and ran unopposed in the Nov 4 2025 general; new term seated Jan 2026. Widow of the late Rep. A. Donald McEachin (VA-04), who died Nov 2022; they met in law school at UVA and have three children. Life member Delta Sigma Theta, NAACP, Richmond Crusade for Voters. Notable: she publicly DECLINED to join 'Virginia Progressive Prosecutors for Justice,' saying she 'doesn't need to join a club' to make change — her 2025 primary challenger Tom Barbour ran from her left as the explicitly-progressive challenger, asserting 'Colette McEachin is not a progressive prosecutor' (Virginia Mercury, VPM).

candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond Public Schools School Board Vice Chair, District 1, sworn 1/1/2025, term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Fix party Unknown→I (formally nonpartisan ballot; no public D/R registration confirmed). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed; symbolic Jan 8 2025 oath taken with NO BOOK (richmonder.org). EDUCATION/CAREER: U.S. State Department information-security engineer + 10+ years RPS PTA. COALITION SIGNALS: REGS-endorsed (REGS is a governance/equity-focused PAC critics accused of being a charter-school advocacy front; REGS denies); personally supported by outgoing progressive District 1 member Elizabeth Doerr (who sponsored the 2022 anti-Youngkin trans resolution); unanimous Vice Chair pick of the new 2024-elected majority. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: voted NO on the March 2026 motion to add $3M Virtual Academy funding to the RPS budget proposal (motion passed 6-2 over his minority position; quoted 'An additional $3 million may be a difficult ask in this environment,' vpm.org 3/3/2026); voted YES on the June 2026 unanimous ~$4M FY27 RPS budget deficit cuts (incl. teacher-stipend cuts + closing the Virtual Academy, vpm.org 6/3/2026); separately charter-skeptical per Richmonder quote — needs 'to be convinced that data supports the approach of having a charter school in Richmond right now' and 'opening a new school that competes for dollars and teachers is not the first priority' (richmonder.org). Multi-cycle fiscal-restraint signal — direct vote against budget growth + active deficit-closing. HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: campaign platform explicitly opposes parental/governmental review of library content — teachers should not be 'search[ed for] subversive messages' in their classrooms and should have 'freedom to use age-appropriate literature they believe will best prepare students for life beyond graduation' and 'impart truthful lessons without fear of being fired' (vpm.org elections 2024-11-01 Q&A). Explicit anti-parental-rights / anti-book-review framing — the inverse of family q2 ('school board has voted to remove CRT/SOGI/comprehensive-sex-ed/gender-ideology materials from district curriculum and library shelves'). HARD FALSE border_immigration q1: voted YES (unanimous board, Feb 4 2025) for the RPS 'Safe Zone' resolution declaring ICE presence on campuses 'substantially disrupts the learning environment' and limiting agent admission to cases where law requires it; RPS was first-in-area to adopt such policy (axios.com 2/7/2025 + vpm.org 2/5/2025). As Vice Chair he was part of the unanimous coalition. Direct sanctuary-style anti-ICE-cooperation vote — rubric-adverse on q1 ('cooperates with ICE detainers + transfers' inverse). HARD FALSE border_immigration q2: same Feb 4 2025 Safe Zone vote also maps to q2 ('voted AGAINST local sanctuary-city ordinances' inverse) since the RPS resolution functions as a school-grounds sanctuary policy. HARD FALSE refuse_state_overreach q3: April 2025 RPS budget discussion — Percival expressed concern about RPS being trapped on 'Comprehensive Support and Improvement' status IF Trump dismantles the U.S. Department of Education (vpm.org 4/9/2025); his posture is generally pro-federal-DoE-oversight, NOT subsidiarity / lowest-competent-level. Direct anti-subsidiarity quote (rubric-adverse on q3 'Official affirms subsidiarity'). NOTABLE QUOTES: at Jan 8 2025 organizational meeting — 'the real story here tonight is consensus and collaboration'; on collective bargaining — compared changes to 'a one-size-fits-all sweater not designed to flatter anybody but to keep everyone warm'; campaign joke / platform — 'Make School Board Boring Again' buttons. 1 TRUE / 4 FALSE on cited cells = 20% — coherent moderate-progressive consensus / governance-focused first-term Vice Chair w/ State Department IT background, fiscally restrained but on all culture-war markers aligned with the prevailing progressive-RPS-coalition direction. Recommend revisiting after FY28 budget cycle and any 2027 curriculum / library-content votes.

candidates.name: Anne Holton
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond Public Schools Board, District 6 (officially nonpartisan, real-world firm Democratic alignment — former VA SecEd under McAuliffe, wife of Sen. Tim Kaine, daughter of moderate-R Gov. Linwood Holton but her own career is firmly Democratic). Interim-appointed Feb 3 2026 by 7-0-1 board vote to fill the Shonda Harris-Muhammed seat; running for the seat in Nov 2026 special. RELIGION CORRECTION: Catholic at St. Elizabeth Richmond (not Episcopalian as I speculated). EDUCATION CORRECTION: Princeton + Harvard Law (not W&M / UVA). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q0: as VA Secretary of Education 2014-2016 'pushed back against the expansion of charter schools' in Virginia and advocated for teacher pay increases instead (WVTF Jul 28 2016, Wash Post); on VA Board of Education Apr 25 2024 was the lone NO vote on the Roanoke College lab school program and four others, direct quote: 'I'm concerned that, in our haste to get this done quickly, we're lowering the standards for what we've seen in the rest of lab schools, just to get it done fast.' HARD multi-vector evidence of opposition to school-choice expansion. HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: on VA BOE 2022-2023 objected to Youngkin's draft history standards, called the November 2022 draft 'a disaster,' noted Reagan-5x-vs-Obama-0x asymmetry, said 'Teachers have to walk gingerly around anything that connects what happens today with the past'; joined Board President Daniel Gecker and VP Tammy Mann (Northam/McAuliffe appointees) opposing the introductory pages as 'political' (Virginia Mercury, WDBJ7). HARD-direction adverse on the CRT / standards-restriction rubric cell. SOFT FALSE biblical_marriage q4 + family_child_sovereignty q1 + refuse_state_overreach q0: Aug 29 2024 X thread (status 1829241205441311103) + Blue Virginia called the Youngkin BOE's 'changes' — which centered on parental notification, biological-sex / model-policy reversal, and conservative history standards — 'terrible changes that are bad for VA students, parents, and educators' (HARD direction-adverse statement on the policies the cited cells protect). HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: Mar 2-3 2026 RPS FY27 budget Holton and VP Matthew Percival cast the 2 NO votes in the 6-2 board approval of the ~$549.9M Avula-proposed package, arguing it was 'unrealistic' to expect additional funds with city property-tax freezes coming (VPM Mar 3 2026, Axios Richmond Mar 2 2026); also voted against funding the Richmond Virtual Academy for early-cut reasons. Affirmative fiscal-restraint vote against an oversized education budget = rubric-positive on q2 even from a Democratic education-establishment figure (a meaningful counter-archetype softener captured per skill discipline). HARD FALSE self_defense q0 + q1: campaigns on 'tighter restrictions on guns,' cites 'over 50 [RPS] students [lost] to guns' in seven years and calls the situation 'inexcusable' (The Richmonder special-election article). NOT scored sanctity_of_life / christian_liberty / election_integrity / public_justice / border_immigration — outside school-board lane and no quote-bearing positions located. NOT scored biblical_marriage q0/q2 — RPS Oct 3 2022 transgender policy rejection vote (8-1) PREDATES her tenure (she joined Feb 2026), no direct vote record; her general posture is direction-adverse but rubric strictness keeps q0/q2 null and credits q4 only because the Aug 2024 X thread specifically named the policy reversal she opposed. NOT scored family_child_sovereignty q1 cited cell despite Aug 2024 thread — promoted from null to SOFT-FALSE based on the same cite because the Youngkin model policies center on parental notification on social transition specifically. NOT scored refuse_state_overreach q1-q4: no specific quotes on COVID-mandate-refusal / ESG / subsidiarity / refused-enforcement located. 1 TRUE / 6 FALSE on cited cells = ~14% — coherent for standard Northam/McAuliffe-Democratic education establishment figure with a fiscal-caution counter-archetype softener (the FY27 NO vote). Recommend revisiting after the Nov 2026 special election outcome and any 2027 RPS culture-war votes.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic — congregant of St. Elizabeth Catholic Church in Richmond, a predominantly African American parish; married Tim Kaine in November 1984 at a Roman Catholic church in Richmond (per Wikipedia, primary biography sources).
profile.education: Princeton University, Woodrow Wilson School of Public and International Affairs, magna cum laude (1980); J.D. Harvard Law School (1983).
profile.background: Born Feb 1 1958 in Roanoke, VA. Daughter of moderate-Republican Gov. Linwood Holton (1970-1974) — at age 12 her father enrolled her at the predominantly Black Mosby Middle School (now MLK Jr. Middle School) to end Massive Resistance to school integration, a defining biographical fact she cites constantly. Career: law clerk for Judge Robert R. Merhige Jr. (EDVA); Central Virginia Legal Aid Society attorney (1983-1998); Richmond Juvenile & Domestic Relations District Court judge (sworn 6/30/1998, chief judge 2000-2003, resigned late 2005); First Lady of VA 2006-2010 (married Sen. Tim Kaine); Virginia Secretary of Education 1/11/2014 – 7/25/2016 under Gov. Terry McAuliffe; Virginia Board of Education member Feb 2017 – ~2024 (appointed by McAuliffe); George Mason University visiting professor (May 2017) and interim president 6/20/2019 – 6/30/2020. Three adult children — Nat (b.1990), Woody (b.1992), Annella (b.1995) — all RPS graduates. Brothers Woody Holton (historian, USC) and Dwight Holton (former US Attorney, Oregon). RPS School Board, District 6 (officially nonpartisan ballot, real-world Democratic alignment) — interim-appointed Feb 3 2026 by a 7-0-1 board vote to fill the seat vacated by resigned Shonda Harris-Muhammed (Dec 2025, departed to be Southampton County superintendent); announced she will run in the Nov 2026 special election (candidacy_status set general_candidate).

candidates.name: Kenneth Cooper 'Kenny' Alexander
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor of Norfolk (officially nonpartisan; D-aligned per lifelong party affiliation + 2024 Harris-Walz Norfolk LGBTQ rally appearance). Term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. HARD positive public_justice q0 evidence: Direct quote in WTKR coverage of gun-violence response — 'My council and I, we are not defunding police. We are funding police.' Plus 13NewsNow (June 2020) reporting Alexander 'do[es] not support reducing the police department's budget at all.' State of the City messaging across 2024-2026 credits adding 43 new sworn officers + 'largest academy class of sheriff's deputies in a decade' for the 20% violent-crime drop. Multi-year affirmative pro-police pattern clears the high bar — this is exactly the kind of D-aligned official who EARNS the TRUE on the police-backing cell per the rubric's principle of giving credit where earned. HARD adverse christian_liberty q0 evidence: in WTKR 2017 'The Mayor's Ministry' profile, Alexander explicitly framed public profession of his Christian conviction as off-limits in governance — 'You have to be careful that you understand that line, and never cross that line, because then you are no better than the Taliban.' He is a Christian minister (licensed and ordained since the 1980s, nondenominational) who deliberately keeps Christianity out of policy; that is documented evidence AGAINST the rubric's affirmative-public-profession standard. SOFT adverse biblical_marriage q4 evidence: WHRO 9/4/2024 — Alexander gave remarks endorsing Harris-Walz at 'Out for Harris' LGBTQ-community rally at MJ's Tavern in Norfolk. Explicit alliance with LGBTQ political activism. (Not scored as HARD because I did not locate a signed Pride proclamation from the Mayor's office — the City's Pride Month communications appear to be administrative/PR, not mayoral proclamations.) NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion-stance statement, no PP endorsement, no SBA endorsement), biblical_marriage q0/q2 (no marriage-definition or biological-sex statement), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — mayor doesn't sit on NPS school board; no comment located on the 2023 NPS 'Get Real' adoption or any school-choice/parental-consent/CRT-SOGI issue), christian_liberty q2/q3/q4, economic_stewardship q2 (no BBA/deficit framing — local budgets are routine governance), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (his state-senate roll calls on SB 731/SB 719 not retrievable in this pass), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4, refuse_state_overreach q0-q4 (no documented friction with Richmond on lockdowns/mandates/ESG/unconstitutional directives — generally cooperative posture), border_immigration q1/q2 (no statement; Norfolk Sheriff Joe Baron operates an ICE IGSA at the city jail but that is sheriff function, not mayoral), self_defense q0/q1 (NO Norfolk firearms-in-buildings ordinance on the books — the 2020 ordinance was pulled by the City Attorney before any roll-call and never returned; cannot score Alexander adverse on that basis). Counter-archetype context: D mayor + ordained Christian minister + affirmative anti-defund pro-police record + explicit church-state separation posture. Verification note: the 'Apostolic bishop' framing sometimes used did NOT verify — he is listed as 'licensed and ordained Minister' per WTKR; not on Norfolk Apostolic Church staff (Sr. Pastor is Michael Blankenship).

profile.religion: Christian (licensed and ordained Minister since the 1980s, nondenominational, non-pastoring; speaks at 'churches, temples, mosques, and synagogues' per WTKR 2017 'The Mayor's Ministry' profile). Explicitly compartmentalizes faith from governance.
profile.education: Lake Taylor HS (1985); A.S. Mortuary Science (Brightpoint CC, formerly John Tyler CC); B.A. Political Science (Old Dominion); M.A. Diplomacy (Norwich); Ph.D. Leadership and Change (Antioch, 2019)
profile.background: Born and raised in Norfolk. President, Metropolitan Funeral Service. Vice Chancellor for Strategic Partnerships, Virginia Community College System. VA House of Delegates 89th District 2002-2012. VA Senate 5th District 2012-2016. Virginia Legislative Black Caucus chair (2008). 25th and first Black Mayor of Norfolk (since 7/1/2016); re-elected 11/5/2024 with 59.3% (def. Thomas Leeman Jr. 33.4%, Giovanni Dolmo 6.6%). Current term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028. Norfolk City Council mayoral seat is officially nonpartisan but Alexander is a lifelong Democrat; endorsed Harris-Walz at a Norfolk 'Out for Harris' rally Sept 2024.

candidates.name: Martin A. Thomas Jr.
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk Council Ward 1 since Aug 2016 special election (re-elected 2022, term ends 12/31/2026); Vice Mayor since July 2018 (re-elected Jan 2023). RELIGION: Catholic by inference from St. Mary's Catholic Cemetery Board service 2010-2017 (not self-declared in public statements). EDUCATION: VT public-affairs + Richmond Law. REAL-WORLD ALIGNMENT: Democratic — appointed to VA College Building Authority by Gov. Northam (D), endorsed Democrat Angelia Williams Graves for VA HD-90, appeared in McGee endorsement coalition alongside Mayor Alexander (D) and Sens. Rouse + Williams Graves; no GOP affiliation. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: voted YES on May 14 2024 FY25 Norfolk budget that CUT real estate tax rate 2¢ (from $1.25 to $1.23) + 51% personal-property tax relief on vehicles $1k-$20k + 100% relief on vehicles ≤$1k. HARD TRUE public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR cleared): law-firm professional bio (https://petedecker.com/martin-thomas-jr/) explicitly states Thomas 'has successfully championed increases in pay for public safety employees' — direct affirmative-advocacy self-description; the corresponding Norfolk FY25 budget Thomas voted for delivered a 10%+ raise for nearly 300 police officers (https://www.wavy.com/news/local-news/norfolk/norfolk-approves-budget-with-a-more-than-10-raise-for-nearly-300-police-officers-750m-school-construction-plan/). Direct quote on his policy stance (campaign-bio): Norfolk 'must have a world-class police force' and the city 'needs to pay public safety officers well and create a culture and environment where they can thrive.' Multi-vector pro-police: self-stated championship + corresponding budget vote + first-tier policy quote = clears HIGH BAR. NOT scored christian_liberty q4: agent flagged Dec 12 2023 Armed Forces Brewing Co. 6-1 yes-vote but (a) Thomas issued no individual quote, (b) LOCAL q4 specifically reads 'defends churches from zoning attacks' not generic free-speech defense, so the vote does not map to q4; left null per strict cite-only discipline. NOT scored public_justice q3: agent considered FALSE on PD Chief Boone's holistic-framing budget posture but no Thomas-specific quote located; left null per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty other cells / election_integrity / public_justice q1-q4 / refuse_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense — no quote-bearing positions or council votes located (Norfolk simply did not take up Pride flag-raising votes, sanctuary votes, 287(g) votes, 2A sanctuary votes, or guns-in-public-spaces votes in his tenure that surfaced — the 2020 GOP 2A-sanctuary petition fell ~179 signatures short and the 2020 guns-in-public ordinance was pulled before vote). 2 TRUE / 0 FALSE on cited cells = 100% — sparse-data trap acknowledged but both TRUEs are HARD (FY25 rate-cut vote + multi-vector affirmative pro-police record). This is a textbook 'Democrat-aligned but conservative-direction on fiscal + police' counter-archetype softener and the public_justice q0 high-bar clearance is the genuine cross-track catch. Recommend revisiting after Nov 2026 re-election filing confirmation and FY27 budget cycle.

profile.education: B.A. Public & Urban Affairs, Virginia Tech (2002); J.D., University of Richmond School of Law (2006).
profile.background: Partner since 2006 at Decker, Cardon, Thomas, Weintraub & Neskis PC (Decker Law) — personal-injury, criminal, traffic, civil litigation practice. Won the Aug 23 2016 Norfolk City Council Ward 1 special election; re-elected Nov 8 2022 (79.3% vs Padraig-Eoin Leigh Dalrymple's 19.7%); current term Jan 1 2023 - Dec 31 2026. Elected Vice Mayor by council July 2018; re-elected Vice Mayor by unanimous roll-call at Jan 2023 reorganization meeting (term coterminous with Ward 1 seat). Civic record includes the Virginia College Building Authority (appointed by Gov. Ralph Northam, D) and the St. Mary's Catholic Cemetery Board (2010-2017). Wife Maggie, two sons; Wards Corner area Norfolk resident. Officially nonpartisan ballot — real-world Democratic alignment (Northam appointment + endorsed Democrat Angelia Williams Graves for VA HD-90 + appeared at McGee endorsement event with Mayor Alexander).

candidates.name: Courtney R. Doyle
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk Council Ward 2, sworn July 1, 2018; re-elected unopposed Nov 2022; term ends 12/31/2026 and she is running Nov 2026. RELIGION: Catholic at Sacred Heart Norfolk (per campaign about page). EDUCATION: UMD undergrad + ODU MBA. CONFIRMED DEM-aligned despite I-nonpartisan ballot — Progressive Voters Guide endorsed her 2022 with note 'support for pandemic safety' + climate-leadership credit; ballot remains officially nonpartisan. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4: Dec 12, 2023 Armed Forces Brewing Co. council debate — Doyle stated promotional videos and social-media tied to the brewery 'do not respect the LGBTQ community, women or our Norfolk values,' explicitly treating LGBTQ status as a constituent of 'Norfolk values' (the rubric's state-endorsement-of-LGBTQ-identity adverse direction). Norfolk runs an annual city Pride Month program with LGBTQ Community Liaison office; Doyle has not publicly dissented from any Pride proclamation in seven years of council service. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: voted YES on May 14, 2024 FY25 budget that CUT the real estate tax rate 2¢ (from $1.25 to $1.23 per $100) + 51% personal-property tax relief on vehicles $1k-$20k + 100% relief on vehicles ≤$1k. FY26 budget (May 12, 2026) maintained the $1.23 rate, vote 7-1, she was on the YES majority. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: campaign Safe Neighborhoods page (https://courtneydoyle.com/safe-neighborhoods/) describes her approach to gun violence as 'using the public education system, recreation centers, libraries, and community groups to impress upon youth that guns are not the solution' — textbook root-causes / public-health framing (adverse on q3). HARD FALSE self_defense q1: same page advocates for 'constructive and meaningful gun legislation that prevents guns from getting into the hands of those who shouldn't possess a gun' — affirmative gun-control framing (adverse on q1's red-flag / AWB / registry direction). SOFT FALSE refuse_state_overreach q2: signature platform issue is climate / sea-level-rise leadership ('positioned our city as a leader in combating climate challenges,' campaign flooding page; Progressive Voters Guide credit on 'climate change safety measures' is endorsement-bearing evidence on the ESG-embrace direction). NOT scored christian_liberty q0: she voted YES on Armed Forces Brewing Co. 6-1 explicitly on content-neutral free-speech grounds (her direct quote: 'I took an oath to uphold the right to free speech despite the fact I disagree with the speech'), but LOCAL q0 specifically reads as Christian-business religious-liberty defense — Armed Forces Brewing is military-themed not religious, so the vote does not map and the cell stays null per strict cite-only discipline (the Dec 2023 cite is preserved in the cross-cutting biblical_marriage q4 note above as the LGBTQ-as-Norfolk-value evidence). NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR not cleared): Sheriff Joe Baron is listed as a personal endorser on her endorsements page but a single sheriff endorsement ≠ FOP endorsement; campaign Safe Neighborhoods page lists 'increased pay for Norfolk's public-safety team members ... to resolve recruitment and retention challenges' but this is routine governance budget language not affirmative pro-cop advocacy (the FXBG high-bar rule). NOT scored border_immigration q1/q2: Norfolk has no 287(g); no individually-attributed Doyle vote on sanctuary status located; ACLU-VA has pressured the Sheriff but the council-level posture remains undefined → null per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / christian_liberty q2-q4 / election_integrity / refuse_overreach q0,q1,q3,q4 / family_child_sovereignty / biblical_marriage q0 q2 — no quote-bearing positions located. 1 TRUE / 4 FALSE on cited cells = 20% — coherent center-left urban Democratic council member with one fiscal-restraint TRUE on the FY25 rate cut. Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget vote, any 2026 forum positions, and a focused dig into Norfolk meeting minutes for individual sanctuary / library-content votes.

candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk City Council Ward 3 (officially nonpartisan, D-aligned by endorsement pattern). No,' stating she wanted Norfolk to create a non-police mental-health crisis team and would support reallocating budget funds to establish it. That is the textbook soft-defund framing the rubric scores as FALSE on the affirmative-police-backing cell. profile.religion: Catholic (member with wife Djuna of the Basilica of St. Mary of the Immaculate Conception, Norfolk parish of the Diocese of Richmond, per official city bio)
profile.education: Norfolk State University alumnus; M.S. Management, Troy University (1998)
profile.background: Norfolk native (grew up in Huntersville/Norview/Ballantine neighborhoods). U.S. Air Force veteran (Operation Desert Shield/Desert Storm). Bail bondsman (small-business owner, 20+ years). Former adjunct professor at Norfolk State University (School of Business; School of Hospitality & Tourism Management). Personal story: dropped out after 10th grade, arrested at 17 and spent time in juvenile detention, earned GED, joined Air Force Reserve, went back to college. Great-great grandson of Rev. Richard G.L. Paige (early Black member of the Virginia General Assembly, 1871). Married 25+ years to Djuna; Kappa Alpha Psi (1992); board member Eggleston Services and Revive Community of Virginia; living organ donor. Norfolk City Council Ward 4 (officially nonpartisan; ran and serves as Independent), first elected 11/8/2022 with ~54% over Phillip Hawkins Jr (I) and Erik Barrett (D, 7.4%) — succeeded 30-year incumbent Paul Riddick. Term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 (ON Nov 2026 ballot). Qualified for re-election 3/10/2026; per the Norfolk Office of Elections 'List of Qualified Local Candidates' (5/20/2026), no challenger has yet qualified for Ward 4 (filing deadline 6/16/2026).

candidates.name: Jeremy D. McGee
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk Council Superward 6, sworn 1/1/2025, term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. RELIGION: lay 'home preacher' Christian per PVG candidate entry (no denominational specifics in sources). REAL-WORLD ALIGNMENT: Independent on the ballot but coalition is clearly Democratic — endorsed by Gov. Ralph Northam, Sen. Aaron Rouse, Sen. Angelia Williams Graves, Del. Phil Hernandez, Mayor Kenneth Alexander (all D), former Del. Jay Jones, former Del. Paula Miller (D), and EAN-PACE (teacher union); Progressive Voters Guide named him the more progressive choice in his race (with reservations about his tax-cut stance). Notably no GOP or single-issue conservative endorsements. DEVIATION FLAG: significant Christian-identity and fiscal-restraint signals counter the D-coalition pattern — 'home preacher' designation + Navy veteran + explicit support for the recent property-tax-rate reduction + YIMBY/Realtors/Chamber business backing = honest counter-archetype softener. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: campaign Housing page (https://www.mcgeefornorfolk.com/housing) states 'I supported the recent property tax rate reduction and believe we should continue to carefully consider the impact of property taxes on housing affordability' (the May 14 2024 FY25 2¢ rate cut PREDATES his Jan 1 2025 swearing-in, so he didn't cast that vote himself), AND as a sitting member he voted YES on the FY26 Norfolk budget (May 12 2026) that held the rate at $1.23 with no increase (per Norfolk meeting docket). Two-vector fiscal-restraint TRUE: stated pro-cut preference + actual hold-the-line vote. Progressive Voters Guide explicitly flagged 'concern over McGee's support for reducing taxes that could help fund needed services,' corroborating his fiscal-restraint posture from the LEFT. SPARSE-DATA CAVEAT: ALL OTHER 29 LOCAL cells NULL per strict cite-only discipline — he took office only ~17 months ago, keeps a deliberately narrow policy lane (housing, basic city services, business-friendliness, fiscal restraint), and has stayed publicly silent on culture-war / Christian-conservative rubric items. NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR): his stated 'funding, more staffing, and the right work environments for our first responders' lumps police with fire/EMS, no FOP endorsement located (only IAFF Local 68 firefighters + Sheriff Joe Baron personal endorsement, not the same as FOP); routine 'fund first responders' does NOT clear affirmative-advocacy bar. NOT scored biblical_marriage q4: Norfolk has annual Pride proclamations but no individually-attributed McGee vote or dissent located — null per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / refuse_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense — no quote-bearing positions located. 1 TRUE / 0 FALSE on cited cells (=100% on cited but the denominator is essentially 1, which is the textbook sparse-data trap — the 100% reflects research diligence on a narrowly-focused first-term councilmember, NOT broad rubric alignment). Recommend revisiting after his first full FY27 budget cycle and any 2027/28 culture-war votes that may surface.

Serial entrepreneur — built a high-school computer business, founded and later sold a family-friendly dial-up ISP. President and CEO of Radiant Digital (Norfolk-based digital-media firm, ~50 employees nationwide). With wife Malia owns The Inn and Restaurant at Four Eleven York plus several Norfolk real-estate ventures. Currently co-chairs the Mayor's Housing Commission, serves on HRPDC, Erosion Advisory Committee, Coastal Management and Review Board.

candidates.name: Carlos J. Clanton
candidates.notes:
Superward 7 representative.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk City Council, Superward 7 (citywide), sworn 1/1/2025; term to 12/31/2028 (NOT on Nov 2026 ballot). Prior service: Norfolk School Board Ward 3 since 7/1/2018, served as Vice Chair and Chair before resigning for council — that school-board record is where most of his scoreable policy footprint lives. HARD adverse evidence on biblical_marriage q2/q4 + family_child_sovereignty q2: as a school-board member, on May 2023 Clanton voted YES on Norfolk Public Schools' adoption of 'Get Real,' a Planned-Parenthood-developed comprehensive sex-ed curriculum for grades 6-10. Per WAVY/WTKR/13NewsNow coverage of the meeting, 'Get Real' content includes 'gender and sexual identity,' homosexuality, lesbianism, masturbation, abortion, condoms; the board passed it 6-1 (only Rodney Jordan dissented). Clanton was quoted on the record: 'I will be voting in support of this.' HARD adverse evidence on family_child_sovereignty q1: on the Youngkin 2023 transgender model policies (parental notification, biological-sex facilities) Clanton said: 'I support each student. I support the value of parental involvement, but at the end of the day as a member of the school board and education, I have to put children first.' That formulation explicitly subordinates parental rights to board judgment — directly adverse to the rubric's parental-notification/consent question. HARD positive public_justice q0 evidence: campaign-priorities page explicitly says 'Fully fund the Norfolk Police Department to ensure adequate resources'; he was also endorsed by Norfolk Sheriff Joe Baron — affirmative campaign-platform advocacy + sheriff endorsement clears the high bar. HARD adverse self_defense evidence: Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' designation in 2018, 2022, AND 2024 — MDA designates only candidates who have demonstrated they 'will support stronger gun laws' (per skill: an endorsement by a single-issue group is HARD evidence on that issue's cells). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion stance separate from the curriculum vote), biblical_marriage q0 (no marriage-definition statement separate from the curriculum), family_child_sovereignty q0 (no school-choice position; he is a public-school-funding voice but has not explicitly opposed choice — left null), christian_liberty (any cell — Baptist choir membership is identity, not a policy stance), economic_stewardship q2 (no BBA/deficit framing — 'lower property tax rates by increasing revenue through economic development' is conventional municipal language), election_integrity (any cell), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4, refuse_state_overreach (the 'children first' framing on Youngkin policy cuts AGAINST the rubric's conservative-local-control intent and is already used on family_child_sovereignty q1; the cell about local control against unconstitutional state mandates is generally about resisting LIBERAL state mandates and Clanton has not done that — left null), border_immigration (any cell). Counter-archetype context: faith-active Baptist + Sheriff Baron endorsement + 'Fully fund NPD' — yet voted YES on the Planned-Parenthood-authored sex-ed curriculum and triple-MDA Gun Sense designee. The school-board record dominates the cultural cells.

profile.education: Maury High School (Norfolk); B.S. in Training with Leadership minor, Old Dominion University; Nonprofit Management certificate from Tidewater Community College; pursuing M.P.A.
profile.background: Norfolk native, raised in Lambert's Point and Park Place; eldest of three, raised by a single mother. Director, Norfolk Division, Hampton Roads Chamber of Commerce (first African American, youngest in role); Executive Director, Office of Community Engagement and Strategic Partnerships; Sr. Director of Community Engagement, Hampton Roads Community Action Program. 9th President, National Urban League Young Professionals; former NUL Trustee. Professional vocalist with Virginia Opera ~35 years. Prior elected service: Norfolk City School Board, Ward 3 (7/1/2018 - resigned for council); served as Vice Chair and Chair of the school board. Concurrent appointments: Mayor's Housing Commission Co-Chair; Mayor's St. Paul's Advisory Commission Co-Chair; Hampton Roads Planning District Commission; VisitNorfolk Executive Committee. Norfolk City Council, Superward 7 (officially nonpartisan, but ran with overwhelmingly D-aligned endorsement stack — Sens. Lucas/Rouse/Williams Graves, Dels. Feggans/Askew/Hernandez/Hayes, Norfolk Commonwealth's Attorney Ramin Fatehi), term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 (NOT on Nov 2026 ballot; won the Nov 2024 5-way + Dec 2024 recount by 92 votes over Phillip Hawkins).

candidates.name: Ramin Fatehi
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk Commonwealth's Attorney (elected, partisan D). Second term 1/1/2026-12/31/2029 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. HARD adverse sanctity_of_life q0+q1 evidence: Fatehi is a confirmed co-signatory of the Sept 2022 Fair and Just Prosecution joint statement of 85 elected prosecutors refusing to prosecute abortion-related conduct. Direct quote on 13NewsNow: 'As the Commonwealth's Attorney for the City of Norfolk, our chief minister of justice, I will never prosecute a person who seeks or has an abortion that would have been legal under Roe… what is right has not changed.' That is categorical declination of any future VA abortion restrictions — clears the rubric bar for FALSE on both the public-profession cell and the affirmative-CA-action cell. candidates.notes:
Took office Jan 1, 2025. Former Vice Mayor. Former firefighter. Succeeded Donnie Tuck who did not seek re-election. Term expires Dec 2028.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor of Hampton; elected Nov 5, 2024 (~70% over Richelle Wallace); sworn in Jan 8, 2025; current term ends Dec 31, 2028; next election 2028. Hampton officially nonpartisan; filed as Independent (VPAP committee 'Gray for Hampton City Mayor – Jimmy'). HARD evidence: (1) On MAY 30, 2025 issued a FORMAL LETTER on Hampton city letterhead rejecting the U.S. Department of Homeland Security's placement of Hampton on the federal 'sanctuary jurisdictions' list. Hampton.gov posted the scanned PDF (CreationDate Fri May 30 16:23:13 2025 EDT, Ricoh IM C4510 scanner). Gray on the record (WTKR): 'Our city council has not adopted any ordinances, policies or resolutions, classifying ourselves as a sanctuary city... our police department and the city are cooperating when [we] are requested to assist with any execution of warrants or other activities involving Homeland Security agents working in our city.' → border_immigration q2 TRUE (affirmative rejection of sanctuary policy + endorsed federal-preemption posture). (2) Joint press briefing with Hampton Police Chief Jimmie Wideman on the surge in violence — Mayor Gray publicly partnered with the Chief, told residents to 'lock up your guns around your house, so if you have a break in, they can't steal your weapons,' and praised state-police partnership on 'street interdiction and traffic enforcement' as 'very effective' at getting 'guns and drugs off the street.' Combined with FY26 budget priorities to fund competitive police salaries — affirmative pro-LE advocacy bar met → public_justice q0 TRUE. (3) FY26 Hampton budget May 2025 ($677.9M, 1¢ real-estate tax-rate cut to $1.14/$100, 6-1 with Campbell the lone NO) — Gray was in the YES majority and publicly defended pay raises for city employees; this is routine governance with growing topline spending (5.4%), not fiscal-restraint advocacy in the rubric q2 sense → null on economic_stewardship q2 per the Wolfenbarger high-bar precedent. (4) April 2026 camping/sleeping-on-public-property ordinance (5-2 YES, with Brown/Ferebee NO) — Gray voted YES and framed it as continuing to be 'the compassionate community we strive to be'; he said the ordinance 'would be applied evenly to anyone in violation' — public-order ordinance, NOT affirmative pro-LE advocacy by itself. DEVIATION FLAGS: (a) Gray serves on the boards of Providence Bible College & Theological Seminary AND Bernadine Franciscan Sisters Foundation — religious-board service is real but does not by itself satisfy christian_liberty q0 (high-bar public-square Christian profession requires an active public profession like Anton Bell's Sixth Commandment court argument; Gray has not made one in available sources) → christian_liberty cells null. (b) Hampton city government earned a 100/100 HRC Municipal Equality Index score and sponsors the Phabulous Phoebus Pride Festival — Gray has NOT personally signed a Pride proclamation that is documented in available sources; the sponsorship is institutional/CVB-led, so biblical_marriage and christian_liberty q4 are null pending a direct personal-signature finding. (c) On Feb 27, 2026 Gray accepted the Virginia Center for Inclusive Communities (Peninsula) Humanitarian Award and framed his work as 'building bridges, creating space at the table, and ensuring that no child, and no adult, feels unseen or unheard' — diversity/inclusion language but specifically racial/ethnic/religious framing, not LGBTQ-specific → null. (d) State of the City Dec 3, 2025 'Level Up' speech praised Hampton City Schools' graduation rates; not a curriculum/parental-rights statement → null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 (no marriage/sexuality policy statement and no personally signed Pride proclamation located), family_child_sovereignty (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT/SOGI statement), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (board service to religious institutions is biographical, not active public-square advocacy), economic_stewardship q2 (routine budget YES with 5.4% growth not fiscal-restraint advocacy), election_integrity, public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (no bail-reform/restitution/public-health-framing/sword-of-state statement; Gray's gun-violence framing is shared-responsibility, not specifically magistrate-sword), refuse_state_overreach (chairs VML Legislative Committee but no specific Dillon-Rule pushback documented), border_immigration q1 (no statement on deportation of minors), self_defense (gun-violence framing emphasizes secure storage but no 2A-rights affirmation and no anti-2A position either).

profile.background: James 'Jimmy' Gray Jr.; age ~69 at time of 2024 election. Retired public-safety executive: former Hampton Fire Chief (6 years) and former Hampton Assistant City Manager for Public Safety & Human Services (retired 2014); 26 prior years with Chesapeake Fire Department. Four children, three grandchildren. Affiliations: NAACP Hampton Branch, Sigma Pi Phi (the Boulé). Civic: chairs the Virginia Municipal League Legislative Committee; long Hampton-Newport News regional service. Prior Hampton City Council member 2016, 2018, 2022 terms; Vice Mayor 2018-2024. Elected Mayor of Hampton Nov 5, 2024 over retired Hampton fire captain Richelle Wallace ~70%-29%. Sworn in Jan 8, 2025 at Hampton Roads Convention Center. Current term Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2028. Hampton elections are officially nonpartisan; he filed and ran as Independent (VPAP). Not up again until 2028. Distinguished from any other 'Jimmy/James Gray.'

candidates.name: Hope L. Harper
candidates.notes:
At-large council member.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Hampton City Council member, second term Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2028; re-elected Nov 5, 2024 leading 9 candidates with 18.62%. Hampton council is officially nonpartisan; she ran as Independent on the ballot. THIN ISSUE FOOTPRINT — campaign site (hope4hampton.org) has no policy/Issues page; WAVY-stated priorities are 'more opportunities for youth and families,' 'lowering gun violence,' and 'adding more employment' — generic. RELIGIOUS IDENTITY: member of Bethel Temple Church, an Assemblies of God Pentecostal congregation; church affiliation is on her official Hampton.gov bio. INSTITUTIONAL ROLES: serves on Hampton Planning Commission AND the Citizens Police Advisory Group AND Hampton Redevelopment & Housing Authority — an institutional-insider profile. DOCUMENTED VOTES: (a) FY26 Hampton budget May 2025 ($677.9M, 1¢ real-estate tax-rate cut from $1.15 → $1.14/$100, 6-1 with Campbell the lone NO) — Harper was in the 6-vote YES majority by elimination; routine governance vote that includes a tax cut already engineered by staff is NOT scored as fiscal-restraint advocacy per the rubric high-bar (consistent with the Wolfenbarger precedent). (b) April 2026 camping/sleeping-on-public-property ordinance passed 5-2 (Class 2 misdemeanor); Vice Mayor Brown and CM Ferebee were the named NOs — Harper was in the YES majority by elimination, but this is a public-order ordinance, not affirmative pro-LE advocacy per the rubric q0 bar. (c) Hampton University polling-site approval (unanimous council) — ballot-access expansion, not an integrity measure. ON GUN VIOLENCE (per WAVY 2024 candidate profile): she framed it as 'a national issue that has impacted [our] communities greatly' and called for 'a community effort' and 'proven methods' — vague community-effort framing, not affirmative pro-LE advocacy and not enough to flag the rubric's public-health framing under q3. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Hampton sponsors Phabulous Phoebus Pride and has an LGBTQ+ liaison, but Harper has no individual vote/statement on a proclamation or trans policy), family_child_sovereignty (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT/SOGI statement; Hampton schools are a separate elected body), christian_liberty (Bethel Temple AG membership is identity, not a public-square Christian-symbols/prayer/conscience-rights statement), economic_stewardship (routine YES on a 6-1 budget with a rate cut already in the package is not fiscal-restraint advocacy in the rubric q2 sense — distinguish from Campbell's lone-NO dissent demanding more cuts), election_integrity, public_justice (institutional CPAG service and inferred YES on camping ordinance are not affirmative pro-LE advocacy per the high bar), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Hampton rejected DHS sanctuary designation in May 2025 via Mayor Gray's letter, but Harper made no individual statement), self_defense.

candidates.name: Michelle Ferebee
candidates.notes:
At-large council member.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Hampton City Council member, current term Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2026 (special-election remainder of Jimmy Gray's seat after Gray moved up to Mayor); won Nov 5, 2024 special election with 57.67%. Hampton council is officially nonpartisan; ballot label Independent. Will be on the ballot Nov 2026 for re-election per her campaign site. RELIGIOUS IDENTITY: explicitly discloses 'Active member of St. Vincent de Paul Catholic Church' on her campaign About page — unusually direct religious disclosure for a nonpartisan Hampton candidate. HARD evidence: (1) APRIL 8, 2026 Hampton camping/sleeping-on-public-property ordinance passed 5-2 — Ferebee was one of the two NAMED NO votes (joined by Vice Mayor Steven Brown); she explained her NO on the record: 'After months of work, it represents a solution. But from our perspective, it's not the right solution.' Hampton NAACP opposed the ordinance. The ordinance is a Class 2 misdemeanor (up to 6 months / $1,000 fine) — public_justice q0 does NOT score her FALSE on this alone because the q0 bar is opposition to defunding/dismantling police, not opposition to a specific public-order ordinance; her NO appears framed in compassion/homelessness terms, not anti-LE terms. → public_justice q0 null. (2) FY26 Hampton budget May 2025 — Ferebee was in the 6-vote YES majority (Campbell lone NO); also asked specifically about grant-funded programs in the March 12, 2025 budget retreat — routine governance, NOT fiscal-restraint advocacy → economic_stewardship q2 null per the Wolfenbarger high-bar precedent. CAMPAIGN POSTURE: Issues page lists ONLY Livability / Technology & Innovation / Coastal Resilience — deliberately apolitical good-government profile with NO culture-war positioning in either direction. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Hampton sponsors Phabulous Phoebus Pride but Ferebee has no individual vote/statement on a proclamation, drag, or trans policy), family_child_sovereignty (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT/SOGI statement; Hampton schools are a separate elected body), christian_liberty (open Catholic identity on campaign site is religious identification but not a public-square Christian-symbols/conscience-rights statement per the high bar — distinguish from Anton Bell's Sixth Commandment invocation in open court which earned christian_liberty q0 TRUE), economic_stewardship q2 (routine YES on a 6-1 budget is not fiscal-restraint advocacy), election_integrity, public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (camping NO is not in itself the contrary of any q0–q4 cell), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Hampton rejected DHS sanctuary designation in May 2025 via Mayor Gray's letter; Ferebee made no individual statement), self_defense.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Hampton City Council member, first term Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2028; elected Nov 5, 2024 with 17.03% (2nd of 9 candidates for 3 seats); sworn in Jan 8, 2025. Hampton council is officially nonpartisan; ballot label Independent; restaurant hosted Hampton Democratic Committee meetings — center-left independent profile. RELIGIOUS IDENTITY: member of Gethsemane Baptist Church since 2012 per official city bio (biographical disclosure only — no public Christian profession from the dais located). HARD evidence: (1) WAVY 2024 candidate profile, on his own record: 'As a victim of family gun violence, I support and believe, there needs to be tighter regulations on gun laws.' Additionally reported he posts 'no guns allowed' signage at his restaurant. → self_defense q1 FALSE (explicit affirmative-statement support for tighter gun regulation is the rubric's contrary position to opposition to red-flag/magazine limits/AWB/registries). (2) FY26 Hampton budget May 2025 ($677.9M, 1¢ rate cut to $1.14/$100, 6-1 with Campbell the lone NO) — Bowman in the YES majority by elimination; routine governance, not fiscal-restraint advocacy → economic_stewardship q2 null. (3) April 2026 camping ordinance 5-2 — Bowman in the YES majority by elimination (named NOs were Brown and Ferebee); public-order ordinance, not affirmative pro-LE advocacy → public_justice q0 null. (4) Southern Comfort permit-revocation unanimous council vote (2026) — Bowman quote: 'I'm a firm believer in second chances' (context: reluctant YES). DEVIATION FLAGS: (a) The pro-gun-regulation statement is the single strongest single-cell signal in his record; it is unambiguous, direct, and on his own record. (b) Top stated 2024 campaign priorities (per WAVY): economic growth, business incubator one-stop-shop, attracting chain restaurants/hotels for tourism revenue — pro-business but not specifically fiscal restraint. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Hampton sponsors Phabulous Phoebus Pride; no Bowman individual vote/statement), family_child_sovereignty (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT/SOGI statement; Hampton schools are a separate elected body), christian_liberty (Gethsemane Baptist church membership is biographical, not a public-square Christian-symbols/profession statement per the high bar), economic_stewardship q2, election_integrity, public_justice q0/q1/q2/q3/q4 (no pro-LE affirmative statement; no bail/restitution/sword-of-state position), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Hampton rejected DHS sanctuary designation in May 2025 via Mayor Gray's letter; Bowman made no individual statement), self_defense q0 (no specific constitutional-carry statement located).

candidates.name: Carolyn Campbell
candidates.notes:
1st Term: Jan 1, 2025 - Dec 31, 2028. Newly elected.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Hampton City Council member, elected Nov 5, 2024 (~14.81%, 3rd of 9 for 3 seats), sworn in Jan 2025, term ends Dec 31, 2028. Hampton council is officially nonpartisan; Campbell is publicly listed on the Hampton GOP 'Elected Officials' page (party-in-practice Republican-aligned). HARD evidence: (1) LONE NO vote on the May 2025 FY26 Hampton budget — she dissented because the 1-cent real-estate-tax-rate cut to $1.14/$100 didn't go far enough; quote: 'we just have to balance that out with what the people can stomach'; campaign platform explicitly opposes 'unnecessary taxes and fees' — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE (fiscal-restraint posture documented by a roll-call dissent in the tax-cutting direction); (2) explicit campaign-platform statement: 'Hampton must support the parents' right to voice their concerns, allow an open forum for suggestions, and ensure parents that their children are safe...' Daughter of a Hampton police officer; platform states 'Carolyn supports the integrity of the police officers to keep law and order' and 'Policing in our community needs to be supported with full staffing and resources'; voted YES on April 2026 Hampton ordinance banning camping/sleeping on public property (5-2 majority), saying 'I know this ordinance is not perfect, but it's a start' — public_justice q0 TRUE (affirmative pro-LE advocacy bar met with explicit platform + family LE tie + roll-call vote on a public-order ordinance). NULL on family_child_sovereignty q1 — her 'parents' right to voice their concerns' is general parental-voice language, NOT the specific parental notification/consent on medical/mental-health/gender interventions the rubric q1 requires (per the FXBG high-bar discipline: vague slogans → null). NULL on self_defense q0/q1 — Lafayette Gun Club membership + 'law-abiding citizens' rights to self-defense' is supportive 2A rhetoric but not specifically constitutional-carry-without-permit and not specifically opposition to red-flag/mag-limit/AWB/registries (no Moms Demand / Gun Sense endorsement either; that would have been a hard FALSE — she has neither). NULL on election_integrity q1/q4 — Election Integrity Task Force membership and Officer of Election service are activist signals but do not map to specific photo-ID-with-citizenship-verification or anti-Zuckerbucks rubric cells without a direct citable statement. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Hampton has an LGBTQ+ liaison page and sponsors Phabulous Phoebus Pride but no Campbell-personal vote/statement on a Pride proclamation or marriage), christian_liberty (active Lutheran church involvement is identity, not a specific public-square Christian-symbols / prayer / Sabbath / conscience-rights statement per the high-bar rule), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (no bail-reform / restitution / public-health-framing / sword-of-state statement), refuse_state_overreach (no Dillon-Rule pushback or state-mandate-resistance vote during tenure located), border_immigration (Hampton formally rejected sanctuary label and cooperates with ICE, but that was Mayor Gray's public statement, not Campbell's). Distinguished from Karolyn Campbell (NC, unrelated) and a separate 'Carolyn C. Campbell' candidate elsewhere.

candidates.name: Anton A. Bell
candidates.notes:
Serving since 2012 (appointed, then won special election). Running unopposed for 4th term. B.A. Norfolk State, J.D. NC Central. Immediate Past President of VA Assoc. of Commonwealth's Attorneys.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Hampton Commonwealth's Attorney since 2012; re-elected unopposed 2021 and 2023; current term Jan 1, 2024 – Dec 31, 2027; next election Nov 2027. Currently VACA Immediate Past President 2025-26. CROSS-PRESSURED PROFILE — do not flatten. (A) Bell is a founding member of Virginia Progressive Prosecutors for Justice (VPPFJ, formed July 2020 with 10 other VA CAs including Descano, Dehghani-Tafti, Biberaj) and signed the March 8, 2021 VPPFJ letter to the General Assembly endorsing decarceration-oriented reforms (mandatory-minimum repeal, expanded expungement); Progressive Voters Guide of Virginia 2021 endorsed him as the 'progressive choice... due to his support of criminal reform and police accountability' — public_justice q1 FALSE (coalitional alignment with the rogue-prosecutor / soft-on-mandatory-minimums archetype the rubric singles out). (B) AT THE SAME TIME he is an ordained Calvary Revival Church minister who invoked the Sixth Commandment ('Thou shalt not kill') in court argument during the Bigsby (Codi Bigsby) sentencing and publicly rejected mercy, seeking the maximum sentence — public_justice q4 TRUE (magistrate's God-ordained sword-bearing duty publicly affirmed in explicit biblical-magistrate terms — a rare and significant single data point) AND public_justice q3 TRUE (explicitly refused to frame violent crime in public-health terms; sought max sentence). (C) Office partners visibly with Hampton PD on violent crime, gang, and gun prosecution; bio describes him as a hard prosecutor; no Soros / Justice & Public Safety PAC / Real Justice PAC funding has been located (VPAP shows locally-funded committee) — public_justice q0 TRUE (affirmative pro-LE record meeting the bar without being the Soros archetype). (D) Office runs a victim-services FAQ and Bell explicitly argued for mercy to the victim's family at Bigsby sentencing — public_justice q2 TRUE. (E) Open public Christian profession from his elected-office platform (Sixth Commandment in open court, ordained minister bio on city site) — christian_liberty q0 TRUE. NOT a Soros-funded DA. Bolts noted Bell gave 'a more qualified answer than his colleagues' on parole reinstatement — i.e., he's the most cautious of the 11 VPPFJ members. DID NOT sign the 2022 FJP post-Dobbs abortion-non-prosecution joint statement (no VA prosecutor signed it). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life (Catholic high school + ordained Calvary Revival minister biography suggests possible personal pro-life lean but no public position statement located — null per evidence rule), biblical_marriage (bio describes 'Husband, dad, servant leader' but no marriage/sexuality policy statements), family_child_sovereignty (publicly identifies family breakdown as a primary crime driver, but that does NOT map to universal school choice / parental notification / CRT — null), christian_liberty q2/q3/q4 (open public profession yes, but no specific conscience-rights / Christian-symbols / non-Christian-display statements), economic_stewardship, election_integrity, refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Hampton city formally cooperates with ICE and rejected the sanctuary label in 2025 but that was Mayor Gray's statement, not Bell's — no individual position located), self_defense (hard on illegal gun violence but no defensive-firearm-owner statements).

profile.background: Anton A. Bell; first African-American elected Hampton Commonwealth's Attorney. Career career prosecutor — handled violent crimes, homicides, and child molestation in Hampton; served as Special Assistant U.S. Attorney (E.D. Va.) on the Peninsula Drug Task Force. Appointed CA 2012 after retirement of Linda Curtis; won the 2012 special election and was re-elected in 2013, 2017 (over Nicholas Hobbs), 2021 (unopposed), and 2023 (unopposed); current term Jan 1, 2024 – Dec 31, 2027; next election November 2027. Currently serving as Immediate Past President of the Virginia Association of Commonwealth's Attorneys (VACA), 2025–26. Married to Dr. Dawn Yvette Bell.

candidates.name: Phillip D. Jones
candidates.notes:
27th Mayor. City's youngest directly elected mayor. Ran as independent but strong Democratic ties (endorsed by Gov. McAuliffe, Sen. Locke; member of Majority Democrats). Term expires Dec 2026.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor of Newport News (officially nonpartisan office; the Mayor IS a voting member of council per Newport News Charter §3.04, 'a voting member of the council'); D-leaning per inaugural 'Majority Democrats' membership and McAuliffe/Locke endorsements. HARD adverse 2A evidence: on 11/26/2024 the 6-1 ordinance EXPANDING the city firearms ban (deleting 'openly' so concealed-carry permit holders also barred from carrying in city buildings/parks; only Eley dissented) — Jones was in the 6-vote majority per the charter's voting-mayor rule AND CHAMPIONED the ordinance on the record: 'The safety and security of citizens, of employees, and residents, is top of mind'; 'This is not an infringement of anyone's right. This is … taking steps to protect everyone.' Jones disclosed he himself holds a concealed-carry permit and supported the ordinance anyway. HARD adverse LGBT-promotion evidence: as Mayor, Jones officially proclaimed June as Pride Month in BOTH 2023 ('NN mayor proclaims June as Pride Month; City to host first Pride festival' — WTKR 6/2023) and 2025 ('I AM WHAT I AM: Newport News Pride Festival' — Mayor's proclamation 6/28/2025). These are Mayor's-office proclamations (not council votes), and they're affirmative state promotion of LGBTQ identity. HARD adverse public_justice q3 evidence: publicly framed gun violence as a 'public health crisis' in an Everytown press release ('Gun violence is a public health crisis affecting our nation, and I commend the Everytown Community Safety Fund for seeing the benefits of investing in community health systems'). HARD positive public_justice q0 evidence: repeated affirmative pro-police advocacy beyond bare budget votes — 'We're really doubling down on public safety here in Newport News' (FY27 budget) and 'We have a budget that invests number one in our public safety' (FY25 budget) — clears the high bar. HARD adverse border q2 evidence: joint statement with NNPS School Board Chair Lisa Surles-Law on federal ICE actions in schools (Jan 2025) — NNPS schools 'must remain safe havens'; 'protection of all residents — regardless of immigration status'; 'We will not allow federal immigration policies to disrupt the educational experience or hinder the progress of our youth' — sanctuary-style posture against federal immigration enforcement. SEPARATE deviation context (not directly rubric-scored): Jones authored an op-ed (FairVote, 11/20/2024) endorsing expansion of ranked-choice voting to all VA local elections including mayoral races — cuts against the spirit of election_integrity simplicity, but rubric cells q0/q1/q4 are about paper ballots/voter ID/Zuckerbucks specifically, none of which Jones has addressed; left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no pro-life or pro-choice proclamation), biblical_marriage q0/q2 (no marriage-definition or biological-sex statement), family_child_sovereignty q0/q1/q2 (no school-choice/parental-consent/CRT-SOGI position), christian_liberty (no profession of Christ — Hampton Christian Academy is HS-only, not a personal-faith statement), economic_stewardship q2 (no BBA/deficit framing — the FY budgets are routine governance), election_integrity q0/q1/q4, public_justice q1/q2/q4, refuse_state_overreach q0-q4, border_immigration q1, self_defense q0 separately from q1 (the firearms vote/championing affirms BOTH cells against constitutional carry posture). Religion: NO current church affiliation cited; Hampton Christian Academy HS attendance is the only data point and is not a profession of faith. Counter-archetype flag: CCW-permit-holding USMC officer who sponsors the city firearms-buildings ban — unusual.

profile.education: Hampton Christian Academy (NN, 2008); U.S. Naval Academy B.S. History (2012, vice-presidential nomination); Harvard Business School MBA + Harvard Kennedy School MPP
profile.background: Born 9/19/1989 at Kadena AFB, Okinawa (USAF parents — father F-15 pilot, mother KC-135 navigator). USMC infantry officer 1st Bn/8th Marines 2012-2018 active; currently Major, USMCR. Bain & Company social-impact/public-sector consultant. Newport News Planning Commission ~2 yrs prior to mayoral run. Wife: Jamila Wynter, M.D. Omega Psi Phi. Inaugural member, 'Majority Democrats' coalition (Democratic Mayors Assn); Wikipedia/press categorize him under VA Democrats despite the 2022 independent ballot label. Endorsed in 2022 by Gov. Terry McAuliffe and Sen. Mamie Locke. 27th Mayor of Newport News and the city's youngest directly-elected mayor; term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 (on Nov 2026 ballot — filing deadline 6/16/2026; per WAVY, campaign said he 'will announce his reelection campaign this fall'; convicted former Del. Phil Hamilton (R) exploring a challenge).

candidates.name: Tina L. Vick
candidates.notes:
South District representative. Sworn in for 2025-2028 term.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Newport News City Council, South District 3 / Seat A, in her FIFTH four-year term (since May 2008) — former Vice Mayor 2016-2020; lost the 2022 mayoral race to Jones (3rd, 18.8%). Re-elected 11/5/2024 with 68.8%; term ends 12/31/2028 (NOT on Nov 2026 ballot). HARD adverse 2A evidence — TWO separate roll-call votes restricting lawful carry: (1) On 11/26/2024 Vick was in the 6-1 majority voting YES to expand the NN firearms ban in city-owned buildings/parks (deleting 'openly' so concealed-carry permit holders are also barred — only Eley dissented); by elimination on a 6-1 tally the YES votes were Jones, Bethany, Long, Harris, Coleman (sworn the next month — actually he was NOT yet seated for the 11/26 vote; the sixth YES was Vick herself plus one of Marcellus Harris's first acts immediately after swearing-in OR a councilmember who has since departed — see press for the full roll), AND Vick. (2) She also voted YES on the original Oct 2020 open-carry ban (6-1, with former Councilwoman Patricia Woodbury the sole NO). Two distinct roll calls on local gun-control expansions. Voted YES with 7-0 majority on the FY2026 operating budget (5/13/2025) and was at the FY2027 budget discussion 5/2026 — routine governance, scored null on economic_stewardship q2 (BBA framing). Community work: 'Let's Talk Parenting' event (Mar 2024); Women's Empowerment 'Girls Camp.' On a 2022 gun-violence work session she said: 'I am certainly for giving funding to provide the resources in funding for organizations' — community-organization funding framing, NOT affirmative pro-NNPD advocacy per the skill's high bar; public_justice q0 left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life, biblical_marriage (any cell — the 2025 NN Pride proclamation was the Mayor's, not a council vote), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — 'Let's Talk Parenting' is community engagement, not a policy statement on school choice or CRT/SOGI/parental consent), christian_liberty (any cell — no profession of Christ; 'town halls and local churches' tax-outreach remark is civic-spaces reference only), election_integrity (any cell), public_justice q0/q1/q2/q3/q4, refuse_state_overreach (any cell), border_immigration (any cell). No public church/faith affiliation found. Note for the audit trail: in 2019 a Change.org petition (~200 signatures) by David Wilson sought her recall as Vice Mayor citing 'abuse of power, gentrification … housing violations'; did not advance (13NewsNow, Dec 2019) — hostile-constituent petitioning, not a finding. In 2022 her son Terrance was arrested in a multi-jurisdictional drug bust (federal conspiracy charges; she declined comment).

(3) FY2026-27 budget May 2026 — unanimous; 1¢ cut to $1.06; Bennett said the 1¢ cut 'was not enough' → economic_stewardship q2 TRUE (pushed for DEEPER rate cuts beyond the consensus 1¢; explicit anti-spending restraint posture beyond routine budget approval). candidates.name: William E. Moody Jr.
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Portsmouth Vice Mayor + City Council, at-large (officially nonpartisan, R-leaning Independent by issue profile). Continuously elected since 1998, 7 confirmed terms; current term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026; PER WAVY 5/27/2026 he reversed course and IS seeking an 8th term in Nov 2026 (overrides the 1530 dossier's stale assumption). candidacy_status set general_candidate. HARD TRUE self_defense q0 + q1 evidence: as a long-serving councilmember he was a YES vote on the 1/14/2020 4-3 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' resolution declaring Portsmouth a 2A sanctuary, and publicly defended Councilman Nathan Clark's right to bring an AR-15 into the council meeting that night — direct quote: 'I do not believe that Council[man] Clark needs to apologize for exercising his right as a law enforcement officer to carry his weapon to a public meeting.' The 2020 resolution was explicitly framed as opposition to the then-Democratic-trifecta VA General Assembly's pending gun-control package (red-flag/AWB/UBC/mag-limits) — hard adverse to those restrictions. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2 evidence: 2022 platform pledge 'a safe city with lower taxes' (campaign signature line per WAVY/Ballotpedia); voted YES on Portsmouth's FY25 budget that used Rivers Casino revenue to MAINTAIN a real-estate tax-rate DECREASE from $1.30 to $1.23 — same explicit-tax-cut framing the SKILL credited Mayor Glover for in the 1530 dossier (q2 TRUE precedent for Portsmouth councilmembers who voted YES on that budget). HARD TRUE public_justice q0 evidence (multi-vector affirmative pro-police advocacy, clears the high bar): (1) on 7/14/2020 he personally emailed Police Chief Angela Greene specifically demanding that the law be enforced and charges filed against the Confederate-monument vandals — NAACP attempted to have him criminally charged for the email, but a magistrate declined to issue process; (2) 2022 platform: 'The city is short 80 to 100 police officers… we need to maintain a full complement of 260 sworn officers' and restore the Community Impact Officer program; (3) publicly defended Councilman Nathan Clark's right to carry an AR-15 in chambers as a law-enforcement officer; (4) 2016: called BLM tunnel-blockers 'thugs' (later apologized — captures rhetorical posture against criminal-conduct framing). Multi-vector and quote-bearing — clears the affirmative-advocacy bar. HARD FALSE public_justice q3 evidence: voted YES on Portsmouth's gun-buyback program while admitting on the record 'studies show gun buyback programs aren't effective at obtaining guns from those who will use them to harm others' but adding 'it can't hurt' — i.e. acquiesced to the public-health framing of gun violence he himself didn't believe was efficacious. Affirmative participation in soft public-health framing → adverse on q3. HARD TRUE refuse_state_overreach q0 evidence: the 1/14/2020 2A resolution was an explicit assertion of local control against an unconstitutional state mandate the Democratic-trifecta VA General Assembly was preparing — Moody's direct quote: 'I can appreciate liberals' knee jerk reactions but if they have a problem with the law they should use the system they now control to change it.' Local-control posture against state overreach. Portsmouth, Judge Hammond ruled the council violated FOIA when fining him $1,500 for disclosing closed-session monument discussions — quote: 'good news for anybody who supports open government and transparency') is a clean transparency/free-speech victory but the rubric's christian_liberty q4 cell is specifically about state-funded promotion of non-Christian religious displays, which the FOIA win does not address; no cell maps cleanly, so the FOIA narrative is captured in profile context and left unscored), election_integrity q0/q1/q4, public_justice q1/q2/q4 (no cash-bail / restitution / Romans-13 framing — his 'use the system' line is procedural-democracy framing, not Romans-13 magisterial-sword framing), refuse_state_overreach q1/q2/q3/q4 (no COVID-mandate-resistance / ESG / subsidiarity statement located beyond q0), border_immigration q1/q2 (no Moody statement on Portsmouth's May 2025 DHS sanctuary listing or Sheriff Moore's WSO/287(g) cancellation; his 2019 'Squad' tweet was racial-framing not immigration-policy and is not scoreable). Bryant ('Kitty'), per official portsmouthva.gov page. RELIGION: Episcopalian — Director of Children & Family Ministry at Trinity Episcopal Portsmouth (TEC / Diocese of Southern Virginia, LGBT-affirming mainline parish). Per skill discipline: vocational ministry title does NOT clear Captain-voice Christian-conservative affirmative-profession bar (parallel to how an LGBT-affirming-denomination youth minister can't be inferred to oppose Pride proclamations without a direct quote). EDUCATION: Rutgers Journalism + ODU Secondary Ed. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: her own Facebook post May 15 2025 (https://www.facebook.com/kittybryantforcouncil/posts/122197453532266765/): 'This week, council voted to lower the city's real estate tax rate to $1.24 per $100 of assessed value. This is a change from our previous rate of $1.30… Council unanimously supported this decision.' Direct self-quoted affirmative vote to CUT the rate 6¢ (from $1.30 to $1.24). Reinforced by FY27 budget (adopted May 12 2026) maintaining rate at $1.24 with no increase (https://www.wtkr.com/news/in-the-community/portsmouth/portsmouth-proposes-360-7-million-budget-with-no-tax-rate-increase-but-school-funding-falls-short). SPARSE-DATA CAVEAT: ALL 29 OTHER LOCAL cells NULL per strict cite-only discipline. She has stayed publicly silent on culture-war markers — abortion, marriage, Pride proclamations, family / school-choice, religious liberty (other than vocational employment), election integrity, ICE / sanctuary, and 2A — no quoted position located either direction in 17 months of council service. NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR): public-safety language is generic ('we think about safety all the time'; promotes PPD's Crime & Gun Violence Dashboard); no FOP endorsement, no police-partnership program, no Romans-13 framing — does NOT clear the affirmative-advocacy bar. NOT scored border_immigration q1/q2: Portsmouth appeared on the May 2025 DHS sanctuary-jurisdictions list (later withdrawn) + Sheriff Moore canceled a pending ICE 287(g) agreement, but no Bryant-specific statement or vote located. NOT scored biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4: TEC LGBT-affirming employer context only — no Bryant quote. 1 TRUE / 0 FALSE on cited cells (=100% on cited; standard sparse-data caveat — the 100% is research diligence on a first-term councilmember with a deliberately narrow policy footprint, not broad rubric alignment). Recommend revisiting after FY28 budget cycle and any 2027 Pride-month or sanctuary-policy council action.
